tactical.bear_case: RSI 62 after a sharp multi-week rally into freight rate weakness — spot rates (Drewry WCI) have been rolling over from 2024 Red Sea highs, and a -4% candle can mark the start of mean reversion back toward the SMA50 at $12.16, a ~11% downside gap.
tactical.bull_case: The -4% is a single liquidity-driven ADR move; underlying Copenhagen shares plus a still-golden-cross structure and price holding above both MAs suggests dip-buyers defend $13, with any Suez re-escalation spiking rates and the tape instantly.
tactical.falsifier: Drewry WCI composite turning back UP >10% week-over-week (Suez/Red Sea re-disruption) would flip this to a buy-lean as rates re-accelerate.

positional.bear_case: Massive container newbuild deliveries in 2025-26 expand effective capacity just as Red Sea reroute demand fades, pressuring spot rates and earnings — the golden cross becomes a lagging artifact of a peak already passed.
positional.bull_case: Maersk's integrator logistics strategy and disciplined Gemini network with Hapag-Lloyd could sustain above-trend earnings; sustained price above SMA50/200 reflects a market re-rating the franchise, not just spot rates.
positional.falsifier: A decisive close below the SMA50 ($12.16) breaking the golden-cross structure would flip the positional lean to buy-side (oversold value) rather than trim.

strategic.bear_case: If 2025-26 is actually a cyclical TOP disguised by Red Sea-inflated rates, then 25x P/E is masking peak-ish earnings and reverts toward trough; record orderbook deliveries crush rates into 2026, and 0.71x P/B can fall further toward 0.4-0.5x as it did in prior down-cycles (2016, 2019).
strategic.bull_case: Below-replacement P/B with logistics priced at zero is a multi-year value setup; capital returns (buybacks/dividends), Gemini network cost savings, and any structural Suez avoidance keeping rates elevated drive a re-rating toward book and beyond.
strategic.falsifier: P/B re-rating above ~1.0x (signaling earnings/cycle peak being priced) OR a sustained freight rate collapse with WCI falling below pre-2024 baseline would flip the strategic sign to trim.

tactical.bear_case: 23% above SMA50 with RSI near 68 after a vertical move; any disappointment in semicap commentary or a China export headline could trigger a fast 5-8% pullback from these stretched levels.
tactical.bull_case: EUV/High-NA demand momentum is real and order flow could keep pushing the stock; momentum names like ASML routinely run RSI 70+ for weeks without reverting, so fading strength here loses.
tactical.falsifier: A clean break and hold above recent highs on accelerating volume (or a strong upside order/booking pre-announcement) would flip this to a buy-side tactical read.

positional.bear_case: The price-to-SMA50 gap of 23% is historically wide; reversion to the 50-day alone implies ~18% downside, and consolidations after such runs are common.
positional.bull_case: Backlog visibility of 2-3 years and High-NA ramp give fundamental support; the trend has been a reliable up-and-to-the-right channel and dips have been shallow and quickly bought.
positional.falsifier: A pullback of >10% toward the SMA50 that holds, or a backlog/book-to-bill acceleration, would shift positional to neutral-to-buy.

strategic.bear_case: If 2026 EPS recovery disappoints (China export controls tighten further, memory/logic capex digestion lingers), the elevated multiple compresses and the stock could derate 15-20% even with intact long-term story.
strategic.bull_case: Sole EUV supplier with widening High-NA lead ($350M+/tool), AI-driven leading-edge demand, and durable double-digit growth; at this scarcity premium the multiple can stay elevated and compound, making any neutral-to-trim score wrong.
strategic.falsifier: Forward P/E falling below ~25x (via EPS beats or a >15% drawdown) would flip strategic decisively to the buy side; conversely forward P/E pushing above 45x on flat estimates would justify a stronger trim.

tactical.bear_case: ENB has fallen two consecutive sessions (56.85→55.93) and a break of the 54.8 SMA50 could accelerate selling if the 10Y keeps creeping toward 4.5%+, making the thin 46bps yield spread look even worse.
tactical.bull_case: A modest pullback near the rising SMA50 is a classic support bounce setup for an income name; any soft inflation print or dovish Fed/BoC comment could spark a sympathetic rate-driven rally given ENB's bond proxy behavior.
tactical.falsifier: A decisive RSI move below 35 (oversold on a rate spike) would flip this to a buy; an RSI surge above 70 on no fundamental change would flip it more positive.

positional.bear_case: 46bps spread is near the bottom of ENB's historical range — when the 10Y is paying you almost as much as the pipeline equity with full downside risk, the relative-value case for trimming is strong, especially with 14 straight TRIM days validated by yesterday's drop.
positional.bull_case: The thin spread is partly a function of the 10Y being elevated, not ENB being expensive in absolute terms; if BoC/Fed begin cutting, the 10Y falls and ENB re-rates higher fast. LNG Canada Phase 1 first cargoes and Phase 2 sanctioning are near-term volume catalysts that the spread metric ignores entirely.
positional.falsifier: Yield spread widening back above ~150bps — whether via an ENB price drop or a 10Y decline to ~3.4% — would flip this to a buy. A dividend growth guidance raise on LNG/gas volume strength would also turn the sign negative.

strategic.bear_case: With real yields at 2.16% and the curve only +40bps, a bond-proxy paying 4.9% at 25x earnings offers a thin long-run risk premium; capital is better deployed elsewhere in the book until the spread normalizes, and pipeline permitting/regulatory risk caps multiple expansion.
strategic.bull_case: ENB's LNG Canada and Gulf Coast gas infrastructure positions it for a structural multi-year volume tailwind as global LNG demand grows; the franchise is effectively un-replicable and the dividend has compounded for ~29 years. A rate-cutting cycle would re-rate the entire midstream-as-income complex.
strategic.falsifier: Start of a sustained BoC/Fed easing cycle (10Y trending below 3.5%) or LNG Canada Phase 2 sanctioning with raised dividend-growth guidance would flip this negative; a dividend coverage deterioration or major pipeline obsolescence event would push it more positive.

tactical.bear_case: GLNCY failing to follow a +4.48% COPX day signals the market is discounting the copper beta — coal ESG overhang and weak zinc/cobalt cap upside, so the ADR stalls near $15.78 resistance and rolls over while pure-copper names run.
tactical.bull_case: GLNCY/COPX spread of -4.16pp is exactly the catch-up setup; with price holding above a rising 50d and RSI mid-range (room to run), a single confirming session closes the gap and pushes GLNCY toward $16.50.
tactical.falsifier: GLNCY closing below the 50d SMA ($15.34) on rising volume would flip this from neutral-trim toward a tactical buy-the-dip or confirm breakdown depending on copper; a decisive break of $16.20 with volume flips to clear buy.

positional.bear_case: COPX outperformance may be persistent rather than mean-reverting — if it reflects pure-copper repricing (Freeport/Southern) that GLNCY structurally can't match due to thermal coal dilution and trading-arm opacity, the spread stays wide and GLNCY underperforms peers for weeks.
positional.bull_case: Copper at multi-month highs with tight LME inventories plus tight HY spreads = classic mid-cycle industrial demand; GLNCY's 30% copper EBITDA plus mispriced trading arm means the catch-up to COPX could be a 5-8% relative move over coming weeks.
positional.falsifier: A Chinese property/PMI disappointment or LME copper breaking back below its recent range would invalidate the catch-up thesis and flip the position score positive.

strategic.bear_case: Coal is ~25% of EBITDA and faces structural ESG de-rating and price normalization off post-2022 highs; combined with a P/B at 1.64 (not below book), the cheap-cyclical setup is less compelling than a true sub-0.8x P/B trough, and a global manufacturing slowdown would deepen the trough rather than recover it.
strategic.bull_case: Trough earnings (248x P/E) plus a structural copper supply deficit and an under-valued trading arm mean normalized EPS could be multiples of current — buying near the trough with secular copper tailwinds and a 2.15% yield is the textbook cyclical entry.
strategic.falsifier: Evidence the trough is deepening — a sustained copper rollover below cycle support plus Chinese demand contraction and widening HY OAS — would flip the strategic score positive toward trim.

tactical.bear_case: The +5.13% pop is a bounce within a firmly intact downtrend (below both MAs); momentum assets routinely give back single-day rallies, and RSI 43 has plenty of room to roll back over toward 30.
tactical.bull_case: Sharp rallies from oversold conditions in BTC tend to extend over 5 days; my tactical BUY hit rate is 100% on small sample and today's move could be the start of a multi-day mean-reversion squeeze.
tactical.falsifier: A reversal that erases today's +5% gain and closes red below $36 within 1-2 sessions would flip the tactical read to neutral/sell.

positional.bear_case: Trend intact below both MAs; historical 6.7% hit rate warns the buy reflex has failed repeatedly; 52w low break risks cascade.
positional.bull_case: Deep-discount oversold with uncapped BTC upside and structural ETF bid; +5% rally hints at turn.
positional.falsifier: Decisive close below $33.48 52w low on rising outflows.

strategic.bear_case: If this cycle is genuinely breaking the 4-year pattern downward due to institutionalization dampening volatility/returns, the long-run store-of-value compounding thesis weakens and there is no strategic edge to owning at any phase.
strategic.bull_case: Each cycle has expanded BTC's holder base and legitimacy; sovereign/corporate treasury adoption and ETF rails create a structurally higher floor, making deep-drawdown accumulation strategically sound for multi-year holders.
strategic.falsifier: Evidence of sustained, multi-quarter ETF net outflows alongside declining on-chain active addresses would signal structural demand erosion and flip the strategic sign positive.

tactical.bear_case: Two consecutive down days (-2.63% then -1.74%) hint at short-term distribution; if a broader EM risk-off develops, low-liquidity ADRs like KOF can gap down regardless of fundamentals.
tactical.bull_case: The pullback to neutral RSI from a golden-cross posture offers a clean dip-buy entry; a stable peso and calm volatility regime mean any oversold bounce off the 50-day could resume the uptrend quickly.
tactical.falsifier: RSI pushing below ~35 on accelerating peso weakness, or above ~70 on a peso-driven surge, would move the tactical score off zero.

positional.bear_case: Back-to-back declines totaling ~4% threaten the 50-day SMA at $104.65; a break would void the golden-cross posture and invite trend-follower exits in a thin name.
positional.bull_case: Price holds well above both SMAs with the 200-day rising at $96.67; this defensive compounder typically sees 50-day pullbacks bought, especially with a stable peso.
positional.falsifier: A sustained close below the $104.65 50-day SMA with the 50/200 spread compressing flips the posture to neutral/negative.

strategic.bear_case: Structural peso weakness from a dovish Banxico, US tariff threats on Mexico, and fiscal slippage under the Sheinbaum administration could durably impair USD-denominated returns; staples multiples also de-rate if real rates (TIPS 2.16%) stay elevated, pressuring the dividend's relative appeal.
strategic.bull_case: Nearshoring structurally lifts Mexican formal employment and consumption, KOF compounds dividends and reinvests in digital/B2B (Juntos+) ahead of Arca Continental, and a peso that holds or strengthens delivers a tailwind to both earnings translation and the ADR — a quality EM compounder at an undemanding multiple.
strategic.falsifier: A sustained MXN/USD break beyond ~19 (sharp peso depreciation) or a structural margin breakdown below historical bottler norms would flip the strategic sign to positive (trim).

tactical.bear_case: Below both SMA50 ($320.92) and SMA200 ($315.28) with no base formed; -19.8% drawdown can extend toward the $243 low if the op-margin compression (9.61%) is read as structural rather than transient. Knife still falling — yesterday's bounce thesis failed.
tactical.bull_case: A -19.8% drawdown in the smallest, highest-quality-trajectory prime during an active great-power-competition regime is a gift; RSI 44 leaves room to rally, and any program win or B/B beat snaps it back fast given low float volatility.
tactical.falsifier: A decisive break and hold below the $290 area (toward 52w-low retest) on accelerating volume with no catalyst would flip this to a sell/avoid; conversely a reclaim of SMA50 confirms the buy.

positional.bear_case: 9.61% op margin signals real cost/integration (Aerojet) or program-mix pressure; a drawdown to 44.5% of range with deteriorating margins is value-trap territory, and EPS revisions may be drifting negative, removing the positional tailwind entirely.
positional.bull_case: Backlog compounder with multi-year DoD visibility, trading at a real discount on forward earnings; below both MAs after a 20% pullment in a structurally bid defense sector (ITA tailwind) is exactly the entry the archetype rewards over 20d.
positional.falsifier: Latest earnings call showing book-to-bill <0.95 with negative backlog YoY, or 90d EPS revisions turning down >1%, would confirm thesis erosion and flip this positive.

strategic.bear_case: Op margin at 9.61% with supply-chain (titanium/energetics) constraints could cap the re-rating thesis; if DoD budget slips into CR/sequester uncertainty in FY27 negotiations, the defense bid fades and the cohort premium (even if trailing-distorted) reverts.
strategic.bull_case: Forward P/E discount intact, great-power-competition regime durable, Golden Dome / munitions / Aerojet propellant demand multi-year, and cohort compression toward larger primes offers structural re-rating for the smallest Big-5 name.
strategic.falsifier: A confirmed shift in DoD budget phase to sequester/CR uncertainty (failed NDAA, budget caps reinstated) or geopolitical de-escalation toward a peace dividend would flip this firmly positive (trim).

tactical.bear_case: LIN pinned at all-time highs (99.2%) with RSI 62 leaves zero cushion; any broad QUAL-factor rotation or a single soft semi-capex headline could trigger mean-reversion from a stretched technical perch, and DXY at 120 is a live FX headwind into the next print.
tactical.bull_case: Above both MAs in a confirmed golden-cross uptrend with VIX subdued; quality compounders at highs in a neutral regime tend to keep grinding higher as defensive bid persists, and there is no momentum-breaking news today.
tactical.falsifier: A break below the 50-day SMA ($504.93) on elevated volume, or a sharp VIX spike >25 with LIN underperforming SPY, would flip this to trim-side positive.

positional.bear_case: Position at 99.2% of 52w range means the positional setup is fully priced; a peer catch-up (APD reset under new CEO closing the valuation gap) would compress LIN's premium and remove the relative-value support, turning the spread tailwind into a headwind.
positional.bull_case: AI.PA leading by 1.13pp means LIN is relatively cheap vs its own historical premium; tight and tightening BBB spreads de-risk LIN's large on-site project backlog economics, supporting continued backlog conversion and a structural bid under the stock.
positional.falsifier: LIN/peer spreads widening +2pp in LIN's favor (premium re-extending) plus a break below the 50-day SMA would flip this positive; conversely sustained spread compression confirms the buy.

strategic.bear_case: If the margin compression is real rather than FX-driven — a true mix/cost squeeze as hydrogen project ramp-up dilutes near-term returns — then the premium multiple (34x P/E, 1.22% yield near cycle-low) is vulnerable to de-rating, especially with TIPS real yield at 2.16% raising the discount rate on a long-duration compounder.
strategic.bull_case: A >$1B blue/green H2 mega-project win (LIN is global #1 H2 producer), IRA 45V guidance broadening green economics, or a semi-fab on-site contract wave (TSMC AZ, Samsung TX, Intel OH, Micron NY) would re-accelerate backlog and validate the premium, making today's valuation look reasonable for the structural growth runway.
strategic.falsifier: A confirmed mega-scale H2 offtake/semi-fab contract announcement or an earnings beat with backlog acceleration would flip this strategic score negative (buy); conversely two consecutive quarters of genuine ex-FX op-margin compression with negative price/mix would push it materially more positive.

tactical.bear_case: Price is below both 50d ($412) and 200d ($453) in a death-cross configuration — a -28% drawdown can extend, and a +2.24% bounce in a downtrend is often a dead-cat. RSI 42 is not deeply oversold, so there is room to fall further before a true washout. If the bounce fails at the 50d, momentum traders pile on the short side.
tactical.bull_case: A -28% drawdown in a 46.75% op-margin monopoly with the strongest AI distribution moat rarely persists. RSI rebounding from oversold plus a notable up-day on low realized vol is a textbook mean-reversion setup; mechanical QUAL buyers and buyback support provide a floor.
tactical.falsifier: A decisive break below the 52w low of $356.28 on rising volume, confirming the downtrend continues rather than reverses, would flip this to neutral/trim.

positional.bear_case: MA structure is below both averages and falling — major compounders that break their 200d can stay broken for quarters (see 2022). The 20d forward window for positional has a 42.9% hit rate, below coin-flip. If Azure CC decelerates below 22% at next print, the 'cheap' valuation re-rates lower as the secular growth premium compresses.
positional.bull_case: 21.7% range position on a 46.75%-margin monopoly with ~10% buyback yield baseline is a once-in-a-cycle entry. Trailing P/E 23.5x is a 14.5% discount to GOOGL/META/AAPL cohort — deserved-quality names rarely trade at a discount. Mechanical QUAL factor bid plus buyback floor compound the asymmetry.
positional.falsifier: Azure constant-currency growth printing below 22% with capex still rising 40%+ (ROI questions) would impair the compounder thesis and flip positional to neutral/trim.

strategic.bear_case: Real rates at 2.16% pressure long-duration growth multiples; if the Fed stays higher-for-longer, the strategic premium compresses. OpenAI partnership governance/AGI-clause risk and rising capex with uncertain ROI could cap returns. ~50% non-US revenue with DXY at 120 is an FX drag.
strategic.bull_case: Azure as the primary AI infra rail, OpenAI proprietary distribution, and Copilot monetization inflection give MSFT secular growth ASML-like durability at a cohort discount. If Azure CC re-accelerates above 30% and Copilot seats inflect, the strategic re-rate is large from a depressed base.
strategic.falsifier: A structural OpenAI partnership unwind/loss of exclusivity, or Azure CC sustained below 22% across two quarters, would break the strategic thesis and flip the sign.

tactical.bear_case: The -4.78% drop on a $95 WTI day is a tell that something Petrobras-specific is wrong — a dividend cut headline or Lula interference event — and oversold can stay oversold when there's a fundamental catalyst. Breaking the 200-day SMA opens a path to lower lows.
tactical.bull_case: RSI 31 at the 200-day SMA with WTI at $95 is a textbook mean-reversion buy; my one historical tactical buy hit 100%. A bounce back toward the SMA50 gap is plausible once the headline-driven selling exhausts.
tactical.falsifier: A confirmed close decisively below the 200-day SMA ($15.47) with accelerating volume, or a concrete adverse Petrobras headline (dividend cut/CEO change), would flip this positive (trim).

positional.bear_case: The -22.6% gap below SMA50 reflects a genuine deterioration in sentiment — likely political interference fears or fuel-pricing-policy concerns — and at $95 oil the stock SHOULD be ripping; the fact it isn't means the market is pricing idiosyncratic Brazilian risk that won't resolve on a 30-90 day horizon.
positional.bull_case: At $95 WTI a sub-$16 Petrobras with a 9.4% yield and pre-salt breakeven near $35 is generating massive free cash; once political fear subsides, mean reversion toward the $20 SMA50 offers 25%+ upside plus dividend.
positional.falsifier: Sustained WTI break below ~$70 or a formal dividend-policy downgrade/forced-capex mandate from the Lula government would flip this to trim.

strategic.bear_case: 5x trailing P/E at $95 oil signals peak earnings — when oil rolls over, earnings and the dividend both compress, and Lula's government has a track record of diverting cash flow into below-market fuel pricing and uneconomic refining capex, structurally impairing returns to minority holders.
strategic.bull_case: Petrobras at 5x earnings, 1.5x book, 9.4% yield with a $35 breakeven is one of the cheapest large-cap oil producers globally; if the dividend policy holds, total return (yield + buybacks + modest re-rating) is compelling over 12-24 months versus IOC peers trading richer.
strategic.falsifier: A government move to override the dividend formula / mandate large value-destructive capex, or WTI sustained below pre-salt-comfortable levels (~$60), would flip this to trim.

tactical.bear_case: Spot freight rates are deflating fast as carriers add newbuild capacity (record orderbook ~25%+ of fleet) into softening volumes; if Red Sea normalizes and ships return to Suez, effective capacity surges and rates crater, pulling AMKBY back to test the 200-day at $11.45.
tactical.bull_case: A fresh Red Sea/Geopolitical escalation or a tariff-front-loading import surge spikes spot rates within days, and AMKBY's tight float and ADR liquidity gap up sharply given the cheap P/B starting point.
tactical.falsifier: RSI breaking below 40 with a close under the 50-day MA ($12.17) on rising volume would flip this to a clear tactical trim/positive.

positional.bear_case: The golden cross formed during the Red Sea rate windfall; as those rates mean-revert over the next 1-3 months, the MA structure rolls over and the +8 trend signal proves to be a late-cycle artifact.
positional.bull_case: Maersk's logistics integration (Hamburg Süd, LF Logistics) and buyback program provide a positional floor; with P/B below book and capital returns continuing, the stock grinds higher along the rising 200-day regardless of spot noise.
positional.falsifier: A decisive close below the 200-day MA ($11.45), breaking the golden-cross structure, would flip positional positive (trim).

strategic.bear_case: The newbuild orderbook (~25% of fleet) delivers into a demand environment threatened by tariff-driven trade fragmentation; freight rates collapse below cash breakeven, earnings crater, and the 'cheap' P/B re-rates lower as book value itself gets impaired — a value trap, not value.
strategic.bull_case: Trade volumes prove resilient (CPB World Trade Monitor still positive), Red Sea diversions persist absorbing capacity, and the market re-rates the zero-valued logistics arm toward peer multiples — combined with below-replacement P/B and capital returns, this delivers 20%+ over 6-12 months.
strategic.falsifier: Trailing P/E compressing toward 5-8x (signaling the cycle has hit peak earnings) or Red Sea routes fully reopening with WCI rates collapsing below $1,500/FEU would flip strategic to positive/trim.

tactical.bear_case: A +6.5% day leaves the stock 22% above SMA50 and stretched short-term; a pullback to digest the move is the base case, and chasing here is poor entry. RSI pushing toward overbought after a vertical move often precedes a multi-day fade.
tactical.bull_case: If the spike is fundamentally driven (e.g., TSMC raising AI capex, High-NA orders accelerating), momentum in semis can persist for weeks — RSI 65 is not overbought and gaps on real news tend to hold or extend.
tactical.falsifier: ASML closing back below its pre-spike level (~$1800) on rising volume would confirm the rally was non-fundamental noise and flip me to a buy-the-dip stance.

positional.bear_case: Being >22% above the 50-day after a multi-week run means a lot of good news is priced; any disappointment in Samsung/Intel capex or a China order air-pocket could trigger a 10-15% reversion to the mean, hurting near-term holders.
positional.bull_case: Compounders that go extended in early-cycle WFE recoveries often stay extended — if the AI-driven litho upcycle is just beginning, the SMA50 will catch up rather than price falling, and trimming a monopoly into strength has historically been a mistake.
positional.falsifier: Backlog/book-to-bill turning below 1.0 or a confirmed China revenue collapse would convert the 'extended but healthy' read into a genuine distribution top and push the score meaningfully positive.

strategic.bear_case: If 2026 EPS estimates are too high and the AI capex narrative cools, even ~33x forward de-rates toward 25x — a 25%+ multiple compression on a name that has already run hard. China export-control tightening could permanently impair a high-margin revenue slice.
strategic.bull_case: High-NA EUV adoption and the AI/leading-edge logic buildout give multi-year revenue visibility; ASML's monopoly pricing power means earnings can compound faster than the multiple contracts, and the buyback quietly accretes per-share value. At ~33x forward for a true monopoly, this is not expensive.
strategic.falsifier: Forward P/E crossing above 45x (multiple expansion outrunning earnings) OR TSMC+Samsung+Intel all guiding capex down would confirm genuine peak-hype risk and push the strategic score firmly positive; conversely a >15% drawdown with backlog intact flips it negative (buy).

tactical.bear_case: Price just broke modestly below recent levels (-1.19%) and sits right at the 50-day MA; a clean break below could open a slide toward the 200-day ($50.76), arguing for a small positive tilt.
tactical.bull_case: RSI 45 with price holding the rising 50-day in a golden-cross structure is a textbook pullback-to-support buy zone for a compounder; a bounce here would reward adds.
tactical.falsifier: A sharp 10Y spike (e.g. >4.7%) driving ENB down 3%+ to deeply oversold RSI <30 would flip this to a tactical buy.

positional.bear_case: If the 10Y backs up further toward 4.7-5%, the yield spread inverts and ENB underperforms bond proxies and the broader book — classic rate-sensitive de-rating, supporting a higher trim score.
positional.bull_case: A dovish BoC/Fed pivot or a 10Y rally back toward 4% would re-widen the spread, and ENB's LNG Canada Phase 2 / Gulf Coast volume growth gives a fundamental kicker that pure bond proxies lack — the position could outperform from here.
positional.falsifier: Yield spread re-widening above ~150bps (via either a 10Y drop toward 4% or ENB price decline) would flip this to a buy.

strategic.bear_case: Persistently higher-for-longer real rates compress the multiple on an already-low yield; if Mainline crude throughput weakens or WCS-WTI blows out, EBITDA growth disappoints and the rich 24.8x P/E corrects downward.
strategic.bull_case: LNG Canada Phase 2 FID and accelerating North American gas demand (LNG export + data-center power) drive multi-year EBITDA growth that re-rates ENB as a growth-plus-yield compounder, while eventual rate cuts lift all bond proxies.
strategic.falsifier: Yield rising back to top-quartile of its 5yr range (~6%+) on a price drop, or a confirmed acceleration in dividend growth guidance alongside rate cuts, would flip this to a strategic buy.

tactical.bear_case: After a strong run from $12.19 SMA200 to $15.80, GLNCY is extended ~30% above the 200-day; a copper pullback on soft China data or a DXY bounce could trigger mean reversion in an extended cyclical.
tactical.bull_case: Momentum is intact (golden cross, above both MAs, RSI mid-range leaves room to run); if LME copper breaks higher on supply tightness, GLNCY accelerates without overbought constraint.
tactical.falsifier: RSI pushing >70 with GLNCY decisively lagging COPX (spread turning negative >1pp) would flip this to a trim signal; a clean break back below the 50-day ($15.37) on volume would flip it bearish-tactical the other way.

positional.bear_case: Chinese property remains weak and PMI hovers near 50; coal (25% of mining) faces structural ESG de-rating and price softness, capping the positional thesis if thermal coal rolls over.
positional.bull_case: Copper inventories tight on LME, supply disruptions (Cobre Panama still offline, Chilean grade decline) plus electrification demand could re-rate the copper book; trading arm normalizing toward $3-4B EBITDA adds a re-rate lever.
positional.falsifier: Break below the 50-day SMA ($15.37) combined with GLNCY/COPX spread flipping negative >1pp (GLNCY lagging) would flip positional to positive/trim.

strategic.bear_case: If China structurally decelerates and the energy transition copper demand thesis is slower than bulls expect, earnings stay troughed for longer; coal regulatory/ESG overhang and possible value-destructive M&A (Glencore has flirted with deals) could impair strategic value. P/B 1.65 means you're NOT buying below replacement cost.
strategic.bull_case: Copper enters a multi-year deficit (EV/grid/datacenter demand vs. limited new supply); Glencore's trading arm provides a counter-cyclical earnings floor most miners lack; potential coal spin and aggressive buybacks/dividends unlock SOTP discount where market prices trading arm near zero.
strategic.falsifier: Trailing P/E collapsing toward single digits (3-6x = peak earnings = trim) alongside copper rolling over and Chinese demand contracting would flip strategic to positive/trim.

tactical.bear_case: BTC is in a confirmed downtrend below both MAs (death cross), 25% below 200DMA, and momentum-dominant assets can keep falling once trend breaks. RSI 39.8 has room to drop to 20s; catching a falling knife near range-low support that may not hold.
tactical.bull_case: Price sitting just above the $33.48 52w low with RSI not yet oversold; any flow stabilization or macro relief (VIX low at 16.4) could spark a sharp 5-10% bounce, consistent with my 100% tactical hit rate over 5d.
tactical.falsifier: BTC decisively breaks below the $33.48 52w low on rising volume with funding turning deeply negative — a trend continuation that turns the short-term setup bearish (score positive).

positional.bear_case: Death cross intact, price below 50DMA and 200DMA, making lower lows. In momentum-dominant BTC, below-200DMA regimes persist for months — my 6.3% positional hit rate proves dip-buying here has been a trap, not an entry. Could see $33 break and a new leg down toward $28-30.
positional.bull_case: BTC down 48% from 52w high ($71.82→$37.02) is the kind of drawdown that historically precedes powerful recoveries; spot ETF structure means institutional flows can reverse fast, and at these levels risk/reward skews up with bounded downside and uncapped upside.
positional.falsifier: ETF flows turn net positive for a sustained stretch AND price reclaims the 50DMA ($41.83) — confirming the drawdown bottomed and validating the buy (deepens negative). Conversely, accelerating outflows with a clean break below $33.48 flips me positive.

strategic.bear_case: If this cycle finally reverts to the historical 4-year top-and-bear pattern, month 26 extended_expansion could mark the start of a multi-quarter bear market, eroding the strategic store-of-value premium and triggering sustained ETF outflows.
strategic.bull_case: Structural adoption is still early — sovereign/institutional allocation, potential US strategic reserve discussions, and ETF integration into model portfolios could break the 4-year cycle entirely, making the current drawdown a strategic accumulation window with uncapped multi-year upside.
strategic.falsifier: A regulatory regime shift (e.g., US strategic BTC reserve enacted, or conversely a hostile crackdown / ETF redemption restrictions) that materially changes the long-term adoption trajectory would move this score off neutral.

tactical.bear_case: KOF is up against its SMA50 and ADR may be running ahead of a peso that is fairly valued at 17.2; a quick mean-reversion pullback is plausible if EM risk appetite cools.
tactical.bull_case: Defensive staples bid plus a firm peso could carry KOF toward 52w highs as investors rotate into low-beta EM dividend names amid an otherwise calm VIX 16 regime.
tactical.falsifier: A sharp MXN move (>2% in a day) or RSI spiking above 70/below 35 would create a tradeable tactical edge and flip this off zero.

positional.bear_case: Mexican consumer volumes face pressure from a slowing economy (US-driven trade uncertainty, Banxico still restrictive), and any margin disappointment vs Arca Continental could stall the ADR near highs.
positional.bull_case: Nearshoring continues to support Mexican formal employment and consumer spend; KOF's pricing power and digital/Juntos+ distribution gains could drive a volume/mix beat that extends the uptrend.
positional.falsifier: A close below the rising SMA50 (~$105) on weak volumes, or a Mexican consumer-weakness print, would flip this to the buy/negative side as a pullback entry.

strategic.bear_case: Mexican fiscal/political risk under Sheinbaum, potential US tariff escalation hitting MXN, and structural sugar-tax/health-regulation headwinds on sugary beverages could erode the long-run volume and margin story.
strategic.bull_case: Banxico easing + resilient peso + nearshoring tailwind + KOF's distribution moat and steady dividend hikes make it a core EM staples holding that compounds through cycles, with re-rating room if MXN strengthens.
strategic.falsifier: A sustained MXN breakdown past ~19-20 (sharp peso depreciation) on tariff/fiscal shock, or a structural volume decline, would flip the strategic sign to positive/trim.

tactical.bear_case: The +2.2% is a one-day pop into 50DMA resistance that fails; LHX has been a 6-day HOLD and the drawdown could deepen toward the $300 area if the trailing-P/E premium correction continues. RSI 54 leaves room to fall.
tactical.bull_case: 16% drawdown on a backlog compounder in a great-power-competition regime is a classic mean-reversion entry; the 2.2% impulse + ITA structural bid could carry it back toward the 50/200DMA cluster and onward as defense capital keeps flowing.
tactical.falsifier: A close back below the 200DMA (~$315.65) on rising volume, breaking the support that defines this as a 'setup' rather than a downtrend, would flip me positive (trim).

positional.bear_case: EPS revisions and backlog YoY are unconfirmed; if book-to-bill has slipped below 1.0 or Aerojet integration margin contribution disappoints, the 16% drawdown is fundamental not noise. CR/budget uncertainty into FY27 NDAA could cap the re-rate. Positional BUY only worked 50% historically.
positional.bull_case: FY26 defense budget expansion, Golden Dome / munitions demand, and Aerojet SRM duopoly provide multi-year backlog visibility; LHX as smallest Big-5 prime has the most cohort-compression upside, and a 16% pullback in this regime is where you add.
positional.falsifier: A reported book-to-bill <0.95 with negative backlog YoY on the next earnings call would break the compounder thesis and flip positional to trim.

strategic.bear_case: If the trailing premium reflects genuine forward richness (forward P/E >23x) and margins stay sub-13% on persistent supply-chain/energetics constraints, the strategic case for the smallest prime weakens; a CR or sequester scare would compress the multiple from already-elevated levels.
strategic.bull_case: Forward P/E normalizes the optical premium; sustained great-power competition, NATO 2%+ spending, and Aerojet propellant duopoly drive a multi-year backlog and margin recovery toward 15%, supporting cohort compression and a structural re-rate higher.
strategic.falsifier: Confirmation that forward P/E is actually a discount (<-10% vs cohort forward) alongside op margin recovering toward mid-teens would flip strategic to net buy.

tactical.bear_case: Price at 93.8% of 52w range with RSI mid-50s leaves little near-term upside cushion; any broad mega-cap quality rotation could pull LIN back toward the 50-day at $505 (~2.4% downside) with no near-term earnings catalyst to defend it.
tactical.bull_case: Above golden cross with steady accumulation; QUAL factor remains bid in a NEUTRAL/defensive tape and LIN's low beta means it grinds higher on flows alone, especially if any H2 or semi-fab headline drops intra-quarter.
tactical.falsifier: A sharp RSI spike >70 on a single-name catalyst (mega H2 win) would flip to mild trim, or a break below the 50-day at $505 on rotation would flip to mild buy.

positional.bear_case: Spread vs peers is inline, meaning no valuation edge here; if QUAL flows reverse on a risk-on rotation into cyclicals/AI names, LIN's high relative position becomes a source of funds and the near-highs setup unwinds first.
positional.bull_case: Tight and tightening BBB OAS plus a defensive NEUTRAL regime keeps the quality bid intact; an inline peer spread with LIN's superior fundamentals means the premium can re-expand, and any backlog acceleration would re-rate the positional setup higher.
positional.falsifier: LIN/APD spread widening >+2pp (LIN materially outperforming, signaling stretched relative premium) flips to trim; OAS blowing out >130bps on credit stress flips the tailwind to headwind.

strategic.bear_case: Strong USD is a real, persistent EPS headwind into the next print; P/E at 33.8x trailing is at the upper end of the compounder band with a thin 1.24% yield offering little cushion if rates stay restrictive and EPS revisions soften — classic 'great company, full price' setup that underperforms a re-rating cyclical.
strategic.bull_case: If the ROCE/margin print is optical (goodwill base, FX), the franchise is intact and any H2 45V guidance tightening, EU H2 Bank auction win, or fresh semi-fab on-site contract re-rates the strategic story; DXY rolling over would convert the FX headwind to a tailwind on 70% non-US revenue, lifting reported EPS.
strategic.falsifier: Confirmation that op margin compression is structural (two consecutive quarters of declining like-for-like price/mix ex-FX) would push the score more positive; conversely a >$1B H2 mega-project win or DXY breaking sharply lower would flip the score negative.

tactical.bear_case: The oversold condition is driven by a specific Lula-government dividend or capex headline that has not fully played out; oversold gets more oversold as foreign holders exit on governance fear regardless of WTI. My 33% tactical hit rate warns the bounce often fails.
tactical.bull_case: WTI at $95 with PBR.A 24% below its 50-day is a stretched gap; any absence of fresh political news lets the stock mean-revert violently given 10%+ dividend support and cheap valuation drawing yield buyers.
tactical.falsifier: A fresh negative political headline (CEO ouster, fuel-pricing intervention, forced refining capex) that breaks price below the $15.52 SMA200 on volume would flip this to a sell/avoid.

positional.bear_case: Below-both-MA trend persists because the market is pricing in escalating Lula interference and a structurally lower dividend payout; a sub-$15.52 break confirms a new downtrend and the WTI tailwind never reaches shareholders.
positional.bull_case: A 24% gap below SMA50 with $95 oil and a sustainable ~10% yield is a classic value-with-catalyst setup; reversion toward SMA50 implies meaningful upside as dividend buyers step in.
positional.falsifier: A decisive close below SMA200 ($15.52) combined with a dividend cut announcement would confirm downtrend and flip the sign positive.

strategic.bear_case: Low 4.9x P/E flags peak earnings; if oil rolls from $95 and Lula extracts value via below-market fuel pricing and forced low-return refining/energy-transition capex, the dividend gets cut and the equity de-rates structurally — the EM state-oil discount widens permanently.
strategic.bull_case: Pre-salt is among the lowest-cost barrels globally (~$35 breakeven); at $95 WTI the company gushes free cash, the ~10% yield is well-covered, and any moderation in political interference triggers a multi-year re-rating toward IOC peers.
strategic.falsifier: A formal change to dividend policy (payout cap or special-dividend elimination) or a mandate forcing major value-destructive capex would flip this strategically positive.

tactical.bear_case: Spot freight rates (WCI/SCFI) continue to slide as new mega-vessel deliveries flood capacity in 2026; today's -2.5% is the start of a roll below the 50-day, confirming downside momentum.
tactical.bull_case: A renewed Red Sea/Suez disruption or surprise demand restock spikes spot rates within days, and RSI snaps back from mid-40s toward overbought as Maersk re-rates on rate momentum.
tactical.falsifier: A sharp, sustained WCI spot-rate spike (>20% week-over-week) on geopolitical disruption would flip this negative (buy).

positional.bear_case: The 50-day continues to flatten and roll below the 200-day (death cross setup) as freight rates normalize lower into 2026 oversupply; positional trend deteriorates.
positional.bull_case: Trade volumes (CPB World Trade Monitor) reaccelerate and any Red Sea re-escalation keeps tonnage absorbed, pushing price back above the 50-day and re-establishing the uptrend.
positional.falsifier: A clean break and hold above the 50-day MA ($12.16) on rising volume would flip positional to a buy.

strategic.bear_case: 2026 brings the largest orderbook delivery wave in shipping history; structural overcapacity collapses freight rates toward cash-cost levels, earnings fall sharply, and the below-book multiple proves justified rather than cheap. Tariff/trade-war fragmentation reduces container volumes.
strategic.bull_case: Red Sea diversions persist (longer routes absorb capacity), Maersk's logistics integration delivers margin inflection re-rating the segment toward peers, and continued buybacks at sub-book prices are highly accretive — closing the SOTP gap.
strategic.falsifier: A sustained freight-rate collapse to cash-breakeven combined with management guiding to net losses (P/E inverting toward trough/negative) would flip strategic positive.

tactical.bear_case: After a +3.36% day and 22% extension above SMA50, ASML is over its skis short-term; any soft semi-cap data point or TSMC capex hesitation could trigger a fast 5-8% mean reversion. Momentum chasers are crowding the AI litho trade.
tactical.bull_case: The rally reflects genuine order-book strength and AI-driven WFE acceleration; momentum in secular monopolies persists far longer than RSI suggests, and trimming a +3% day has repeatedly been wrong over the past 29 HOLD days.
tactical.falsifier: A clean break back below the SMA50 (~$1580) on heavy volume would flip this from mild-trim to a buy-the-dip setup.

positional.bear_case: 22% above SMA50 with positioning crowded; a single disappointing guidance print or China export-control escalation could compress the multiple fast given how far price has detached from its base.
positional.bull_case: High-NA EUV ramp ($350M+/tool) and 2026 recovery narrative give multi-quarter positional support; the premium to SMA50 has been a persistent feature, not a sell signal, throughout this leg.
positional.falsifier: Book-to-bill falling below 1.0 or a clear order-cancellation headline would flip the positional view from trim-lean toward neutral/buy on weakness.

strategic.bear_case: If 2026 EPS recovery disappoints (China digestion, memory capex lag), forward P/E re-rates toward the high-30s/40s and the stock de-rates 15-20%; the trailing 59x then looks like a warning, not noise.
strategic.bull_case: AI-driven leading-edge demand plus High-NA adoption could push 2026-27 estimates higher, making today's forward multiple look reasonable for a monopoly compounding 15%+ — strategically a hold/add, not a trim.
strategic.falsifier: Forward P/E rising above 45x on flat estimates (multiple expansion without earnings) OR TSMC+Samsung+Intel all guiding capex cuts would flip this firmly to trim.

tactical.bear_case: RSI drifting below 45 with price now under the 50-day could signal the start of a slow rollover as money rotates out of bond-proxies if the 10Y backs up further; momentum is mildly negative.
tactical.bull_case: A 40-handle RSI on a hold-forever income name with a stable 5% yield is closer to a dip-buying zone than a sell; any BoC/Fed dovish surprise could snap it back toward the 50-day quickly.
tactical.falsifier: RSI breaking below 30 on a 10Y spike toward 4.8%+ would flip this to a tactical buy (rate-driven overreaction); conversely a melt-up RSI >70 would flip to trim.

positional.bear_case: 62bps yield spread means an income investor can get nearly the same yield risk-free; if the 10Y grinds higher the relative case erodes further and ENB underperforms as a bond-proxy with equity downside.
positional.bull_case: ENB's distributable cash flow covers the dividend (~60-65% payout of DCF), LNG Canada Phase 1 ramping plus US gas utility acquisitions are adding contracted EBITDA, and a BoC cutting cycle would compress the discount rate and re-rate the whole midstream complex higher.
positional.falsifier: Yield spread widening back above 150bps (either ENB selling off or the 10Y falling toward 3.5%) would flip this to a positional buy.

strategic.bear_case: In a higher-for-longer real-rate world (TIPS 2.14%), capital-intensive infrastructure with 4-5x leverage and a 24.6x P/E offers limited total-return upside; rate-sensitive income names lag growth and cyclicals, making ENB a dead-money trim candidate.
strategic.bull_case: ENB compounds DCF ~5-7%/yr plus a ~5% dividend = ~10-12% total return with low beta; the LNG Canada export ramp and Gulf Coast gas expansion add contracted volumes, and a multi-year BoC/Fed easing cycle would re-rate the whole sector — a strategic core hold, not a trim.
strategic.falsifier: A sustained drop in the 10Y/TIPS (real rate toward 1%) or ENB yield pushing back above 6% with the spread >300bps would flip this strategically bullish; conversely a dividend coverage scare or major Mainline regulatory loss would deepen the trim.

tactical.bear_case: The -4.24% drop is the start of a copper/China-demand rollover; COPX outperformance may reverse and GLNCY follows the broader miner selloff lower toward the 200-day. RSI has room to fall further before oversold.
tactical.bull_case: A 4% one-day flush with RSI still neutral and COPX surging sets up a mean-reversion bounce; GLNCY is a laggard that typically catches up when copper holds bid.
tactical.falsifier: A close decisively below the 200-day SMA ($12.22) or a second consecutive >3% down day on rising volume would flip this to a sell/risk-off stance.

positional.bear_case: GLNCY's lag versus COPX may be deserved — coal (~25% of book) faces structural ESG/price pressure and Chinese property weakness caps zinc/nickel/cobalt; the discount persists rather than closes. Positional layer is graded POOR (26%) portfolio-wide, signaling this divergence read is unreliable.
positional.bull_case: Copper's rally is supply-driven (Cobre Panama still offline, grade declines, electrification demand); as a copper-heavy diversified miner trading at a discount to pure-play COPX, GLNCY mean-reverts upward with a possible buyback/capital-return catalyst.
positional.falsifier: COPX rolling over and underperforming GLNCY (spread flipping positive) alongside LME copper breaking below $4.20/lb would remove the catch-up thesis and flip the sign.

strategic.bear_case: P/B 1.64 means this is NOT below replacement — the cheap-cyclical setup is incomplete. If China property/stimulus disappoints, copper rolls over and the 'trough' earnings deepen further, making the high P/E a value trap rather than a recovery setup. Coal earnings normalization is a structural headwind.
strategic.bull_case: Trough earnings + copper supercycle (supply deficits, electrification) means forward earnings re-rate sharply; the trading arm provides downside protection rare among miners, and management's track record of opportunistic buybacks/M&A compounds value off the trough.
strategic.falsifier: A sustained copper price breakdown (<$3.80/lb) plus Chinese PMI back into contraction signaling demand-side trough deepening — rather than recovering — would flip the strategic sign to trim.

tactical.bear_case: Price is sitting just above the $33.48 52w low; a clean break invites stop-driven and ETF-redemption selling, and RSI 35 has plenty of room to fall to true capitulation in the 20s before any tradable bottom forms.
tactical.bull_case: RSI 35 in an oversold, beaten-down tape with my 100% tactical hit rate suggests a mean-reversion bounce is the base case over the next 5 days; momentum sellers may be exhausted near the lows.
tactical.falsifier: A decisive close below the $33.48 52w low on rising volume/ETF outflows would flip this to a tactical sell (positive score) as the breakdown confirms continuation.

positional.bear_case: Death cross with price 27% below 200DMA is a structurally broken regime; this cycle may be 'breaking the 4-year pattern' to the downside with ETF flows decelerating, meaning the drawdown extends another 20%+ before stabilizing — exactly what my 6.3% hit rate warns of.
positional.bull_case: IBIT at 6.2% of its 52w range is genuinely washed out; spot-ETF structural demand provides a flow floor absent in prior cycles, and historically buying BTC 25%+ below 200DMA has produced strong 12-month returns once the trend turns.
positional.falsifier: Reclaim of the 50DMA ($41.72) with positive net ETF inflows for a sustained stretch would confirm the bottom and justify pushing back toward max-conviction buy; conversely sustained outflows breaking the 52w low would flip me to neutral/positive.

strategic.bear_case: If this cycle is genuinely breaking the 4-year pattern (no new ATH after halving + 26 months), the strategic store-of-value narrative weakens and ETF allocators could rotate out, leaving a structurally lower demand base.
strategic.bull_case: Spot ETF adoption is still early in institutional/sovereign penetration; a multi-year structural inflow secular trend dwarfs cycle timing, making any drawdown a strategic accumulation opportunity with uncapped upside.
strategic.falsifier: Evidence of accelerating, sticky institutional/sovereign allocation (e.g., major pension or sovereign mandates) would flip me to a strategic buy; a sustained regime of ETF net outflows over multiple quarters would push me to a strategic trim.

tactical.bear_case: Stock has rallied from ~$97 SMA200 to $109; a 1.78% pop on no news plus RSI approaching 60 could mean near-term exhaustion, and any EM/MXN wobble would unwind the move quickly given thin staples liquidity.
tactical.bull_case: Clean golden-cross with price riding above the 50-day, RSI mid-range with room to run; if peso stays firm and EM bid persists, momentum continues higher with no overhead resistance near a 52w high.
tactical.falsifier: An RSI spike above ~75 with a parabolic move on no fundamental support would flip me to a tactical trim; conversely a sharp MXN gap-down breaking the 50-day would flip me to tactical buy.

positional.bear_case: ADR up ~13% off the 200-day; with Mexican consumer facing decelerating remittances and a slowing US-driven nearshoring narrative, positioning is crowded into 'safe' EM staples and mean-reversion risk is rising after the run.
positional.bull_case: KOF continues to grow volumes and pricing across Mexico/Brazil with disciplined margin execution; dividend growth and a sub-13x P/E mean the position can keep grinding higher as defensive EM exposure attracts flows in an uncertain macro.
positional.falsifier: A break back below the 50-day SMA ($105) on weak quarterly volume/margin would flip this to a buy-the-dip negative score; sustained acceleration with margin beats would justify pushing further negative.

strategic.bear_case: Banxico continuing rate cuts plus US political pressure (tariff threats on Mexico) could weaken MXN materially from 17.2 toward 19-20, which directly compresses the USD ADR regardless of operating performance; ~60% MXN earnings makes this the dominant risk.
strategic.bull_case: At 12.5x with 3.76% yield and consistent volume/price growth, KOF is structurally cheap versus global staples peers; a firm peso plus nearshoring-driven Mexican consumer strength and continued dividend hikes deliver compounding total return with valuation re-rating upside.
strategic.falsifier: A clear MXN regime break weaker (sustained move past ~18.5-19 on tariff escalation or Banxico dovish surprise) would flip this score positive; conversely an explicit valuation re-rating catalyst or peso strength to mid-16s would push it more strongly negative.

tactical.bear_case: The -5.45% drop may not be pure noise — if it coincides with a budget/CR scare or a margin-guidance cut (op margin printing 9.61% is genuinely concerning), the drawdown could extend rather than mean-revert. Knife-catching a falling defense name during a CR-uncertainty headline can mean another leg down.
tactical.bull_case: Defense primes at -22% drawdown in a sustained great-power-competition regime with FY27 NDAA progressing rarely stay there; mean-reversion off RSI 40 with the cohort bid structurally intact tends to snap back fast. Single-day -5.45% capitulation is exactly the panic that precedes a bounce.
tactical.falsifier: A confirmed company-specific negative catalyst (guidance cut, major program loss, or B/B printing <0.95) accompanying the drop — that would convert the drawdown from mean-reverting noise into a justified de-rating, flipping tactical to neutral/positive.

positional.bear_case: Op margin at 9.61% well below the 14-16% prime norm suggests possible program charges or supply-chain (energetics/titanium) cost pressure; a 38.6% 52w position with deteriorating margins is not 'on sale,' it's a de-rating in progress. EPS revisions could be rolling over, making the cheap-looking entry a value trap.
positional.bull_case: A backlog compounder trading below both MAs after a 22% drawdown, with B/B historically >1.0 and Aerojet SRM duopoly contributing, is a high-quality name on sale during a structural defense capex cycle. EPS revisions turning up or a multi-billion program award (Golden Dome, NGAD) would re-rate it quickly back toward the MAs.
positional.falsifier: Confirmation that the margin compression is structural and EPS revisions are negative over 90d — that would mean the below-MA position is justified fundamental weakness, flipping positional toward neutral/positive.

strategic.bear_case: If the depressed margins are NOT transitory and forward EPS gets revised down, then the trailing-P/E premium becomes real on a forward basis too — a smallest-prime trading at a true premium to LMT/NOC/GD is a clear mean-reversion-down setup. CR uncertainty or sequester risk could cap the strategic backlog thesis.
strategic.bull_case: Forward P/E normalized for transitory charges likely shows LHX at its usual discount; sustained great-power competition, NATO 2%+ spending, and FY27 NDAA expansion give multi-year backlog visibility. As margins recover post-Aerojet integration, both EPS and the multiple re-rate — strategic is arguably a buy, not a trim.
strategic.falsifier: Forward P/E confirmed above 23x or above 115% of 3Y average with EPS revisions negative — that would make the strategic premium genuine and push the score meaningfully positive; conversely, confirmed margin recovery + B/B >1.10 would flip it negative.

tactical.bear_case: RSI mid-50s near 52w highs can roll over quickly if a macro risk-off hits; the lack of momentum thrust means no buyers stepping up at these levels.
tactical.bull_case: Steady RSI in a golden-cross uptrend with benign VIX is exactly the low-drama grind-higher pattern that characterizes LIN's best stretches — no exhaustion signal present.
tactical.falsifier: An RSI spike >70 on a catalyst (mega-project/earnings beat) would tilt tactical buy-exhaustion-neutral to trim; a sharp break below 35 would flag oversold buy.

positional.bear_case: At 93.4% of range with a 1.24% yield and 33.7x trailing P/E, positional has zero cushion; any quality-factor rotation or growth scare unwinds the leadership fast given LIN's recent outperformance vs AI.PA.
positional.bull_case: Persistent QUAL-factor bid and a tightening credit backdrop keep the golden-cross intact; LIN leading both peers on 1m spread signals relative strength that typically persists for low-vol compounders.
positional.falsifier: A break below the 50-day SMA ($505.99) on rising volume alongside peer spreads inverting (LIN underperforming APD/AI.PA) would flip this positive.

strategic.bear_case: If the margin compression is genuine — pricing power fading as helium/neon scarcity premia normalize and green H2 LCOE gaps stay wide deferring projects — then the +12.5% premium compresses toward peers, a multi-point de-rate from 33.7x.
strategic.bull_case: Reported ROCE/margin distortion reverses as FX normalizes; a mega H2 offtake or semi-fab on-site contract wave reaccelerates backlog, justifying premium expansion. LIN's take-or-pay contracted base makes earnings far more durable than the spot-sensitive flags imply.
strategic.falsifier: A clear, FX-adjusted print showing op margin sustainably below ~28% with negative price/mix ex-FX (true moat erosion) would push me more positive; conversely a >$1B H2 mega-project win with backlog acceleration would flip me negative.

tactical.bear_case: The stock is below both MAs and making new relative lows because the market is pricing a dividend cut and forced capex — oversold RSI keeps printing lower as each fuel-pricing or CEO headline hits; yesterday's -3.15% drop after my BUY shows the knife is still falling.
tactical.bull_case: WTI at $84.65 is healthy, RSI 24 is a genuine capitulation level, and any stabilization in Brazilian political headlines or a dividend reaffirmation triggers a sharp short-cover rally of 8-12% off these levels.
tactical.falsifier: A confirmed extraordinary dividend announcement or a clean upside earnings/production print would flip this firmly negative (deeper buy); conversely a Lula-driven dividend cut headline would flip it positive.

positional.bear_case: Below_both MA structure with a steep -25.8% gap to SMA50 signals a confirmed downtrend; in EM state-oil names these gaps widen on fiscal/political deterioration and the 200-day is about to roll over, turning the flat trend negative.
positional.bull_case: Price sitting right on the 200-day with WTI healthy and a near-double-digit yield is a classic value-support zone; positioning is washed out and any oil or political relief reverts price toward the SMA50 for a large move.
positional.falsifier: A decisive break below the 200-day SMA ($15.53) on heavy volume with WTI also weakening would flip this positive; reclaiming the SMA50 zone would deepen the buy.

strategic.bear_case: Peak earnings (4.8x P/E) at peak oil means strategic downside: OPEC+ unwinding spare capacity could pressure WTI off $84, while Lula pushes higher domestic capex and below-market fuel pricing, compressing the very free cash that funds the 9.4% yield — a dividend cut would re-rate the whole thesis down.
strategic.bull_case: Pre-salt breakevens near $35 make this one of the lowest-cost barrels globally; even off-peak oil it gushes cash, the yield is covered with room, and a politically stable payout plus buybacks compound shareholder returns far above the market's discount for 'Brazil risk.'
strategic.falsifier: A formal management/board commitment to a stable, market-linked dividend policy plus disciplined capex (or a sustained oil supply shock pushing WTI well above $90) would flip this negative; conversely an announced dividend cut or forced low-margin investment mandate confirms the trim.

tactical.bear_case: Spot rates (WCI/SCFI) keep sliding as new mega-vessel deliveries hit the water and Red Sea reopening risk releases absorbed capacity; the stock loses the 200-day at $11.47 and accelerates lower.
tactical.bull_case: A fresh Red Sea/Suez escalation or Panama drought headline spikes rates overnight; with price already below the 50-day and RSI mid-range, a sharp short-cover rally back toward $12.15 is easy.
tactical.falsifier: WCI composite turning decisively higher (two consecutive weekly rises >5%) plus a reclaim of the $12.15 50-day would flip this to a buy.

positional.bear_case: Orderbook deliveries crush spot rates through H2; consensus 2026 EBIT estimates get cut as the contract-rate reset for 2026 annual contracts comes in well below 2025 levels.
positional.bull_case: P/B 0.71 below replacement cost plus a Logistics segment priced at near-zero sum-of-parts means downside is structurally limited; any rate stabilization triggers a re-rate toward book value (~$16-17 equivalent).
positional.falsifier: 2026 annual contract rates settling flat-to-up year-over-year (vs feared double-digit declines) would flip positional to buy.

strategic.bear_case: Structural overcapacity means liner shipping deserves a sub-book multiple indefinitely; ROIC stays below cost of capital, and 0.71 P/B is fair value, not a discount — the SOP Logistics 'free option' never gets realized because DSV-style multiples don't apply to a sub-scale, integrated logistics arm.
strategic.bull_case: Below-replacement-cost fleet plus zero-rated Logistics segment is a classic deep-value cyclical setup; aggressive buybacks at <0.75x book are highly accretive, and any trade-war de-escalation / volume recovery re-rates the whole complex toward book.
strategic.falsifier: P/B sustainably breaking back above 1.0 (rerating done, no longer cheap) OR a structural demand shock (global trade contraction, GSCPI spiking >1.5 on recessionary volume collapse) would flip strategic to sell/neutral.

tactical.bear_case: The +3.31% day and 22% extension above SMA50 leave the stock vulnerable to mean-reversion on any negative semi-cap headline or China export-control escalation; momentum buyers crowding a $1930 print can unwind quickly.
tactical.bull_case: ASML momentum often runs further than mean-reversion models expect; AI-driven litho demand and High-NA EUV ramp can sustain the uptrend, and RSI at 65.7 is nowhere near exhaustion for this name historically.
tactical.falsifier: A close back below the SMA50 (~$1580) or a daily drop >5% on capex-cut news would flip this to a buy-on-weakness stance.

positional.bear_case: At 22% above SMA50 with no fresh fundamental catalyst until July earnings, the positional setup offers poor risk/reward — a normalization back toward the rising MA is a 15-20% air pocket with nothing to catch it.
positional.bull_case: ASML's 2-3yr backlog visibility and 3-5% annual buyback provide a structural bid; an extended position above MAs has repeatedly resolved higher as the AI capex supercycle pulls litho demand forward.
positional.falsifier: Book-to-bill falling below 1.0 or a reported order cancellation would turn the positional read decisively negative (positive score); conversely a >15% drawdown would flip to buy.

strategic.bear_case: If China revenue (which has been ~40-50% of recent shipments) normalizes lower under tightening export controls AND DRAM/logic customers pause High-NA adoption, even forward multiples look full and the stock derates from a stretched base.
strategic.bull_case: ASML is the irreplaceable bottleneck of the AI buildout; TSMC capex guidance is rising, High-NA at $350M+/tool expands the TAM, 2-3yr backlog gives visibility, and the buyback compounds — the multiple is justified by the monopoly and growth runway.
strategic.falsifier: Forward P/E pushing above 45x on a guidance cut, or TSMC+Samsung+Intel all guiding capex down, flips this firmly to trim (more positive); a >15% drawdown with backlog intact flips it to buy (negative).

tactical.bear_case: ENB has stalled below its 50-day MA for several sessions; a break below the 200-day or a back-up in the 10Y toward 4.7% could trigger sympathetic selling in this rate-sensitive name.
tactical.bull_case: RSI at 43 leaves room to run; a dovish BoC/Fed comment or a 10Y dip toward 4.2% could spark a quick bounce in this yield proxy.
tactical.falsifier: A 10Y move of >25bps in a single session (either direction) or RSI breaking <30 / >70 would create a tactical edge worth scoring.

positional.bear_case: With the 10Y at 4.49% and HY spreads tight at 2.63bps, investors get paid nearly as much in risk-free/IG bonds without equity duration risk — ENB's 54bps spread offers little compensation, so capital rotates to higher-spread book members (PBR.A, KOF) on a relative basis.
positional.bull_case: BoC is ahead of the Fed on cuts; if Canadian rates fall and the 10Y eases, ENB's spread widens mechanically and the stock re-rates higher. LNG Canada ramp and Gulf Coast gas expansion add contracted cash flow that the 'expensive yield' read ignores.
positional.falsifier: Yield spread widening back above ~150bps (via a 10Y drop toward 3.5% or an ENB price decline) would flip this to a buy lean; conversely a dividend coverage scare would push it more strongly to trim.

strategic.bear_case: Sustained real yields >2% structurally cap bond-proxy valuations; with ENB already at 24x P/E and a sub-100bps yield spread, multiple compression is the base case and total return lags the book's growthier names over 12-24 months.
strategic.bull_case: A rate-cut cycle (BoC + eventual Fed) re-rates duration-sensitive income equities; ENB's now-larger US gas utility footprint and LNG-linked volume growth could drive EBITDA and dividend growth above the historical ~3%, supporting both yield and a higher multiple.
strategic.falsifier: A clear Fed/BoC easing path that pulls the 10Y sustainably below ~3.8% (re-widening the yield spread toward attractive levels), or a dividend growth acceleration announcement, would flip the strategic score negative.

tactical.bear_case: Below the 50-day with RSI rolling toward 40, weak Chinese property data and a firm DXY could push GLNCY to test the 200-day; short-term momentum is negative after two down days.
tactical.bull_case: RSI in low-40s near support, strong 200-day uptrend intact; any LME copper bounce or China stimulus headline could snap it back above the 50-day quickly.
tactical.falsifier: A decisive close back above the 50-day ($15.37) on rising volume with LME copper breaking higher would flip this to a clear tactical buy; conversely a break below the 200-day would confirm a downtrend.

positional.bear_case: Tight HY spreads and an inline COPX spread mean the easy money is priced; coal weakness on ESG/oversupply and a softening Chinese PMI could pressure earnings and the multi-week trend stalls below the 50-day.
positional.bull_case: Copper inventories are low and the supply pipeline is thin; a China property/stimulus catalyst plus the trading arm's $2-4B EBITDA floor under-credited by the market gives asymmetric upside as GLNCY reclaims the 50-day.
positional.falsifier: GLNCY/COPX spread widening materially negative (GLNCY lagging copper sharply) plus HY OAS widening above ~3.5 would signal demand deterioration and flip the sign positive.

strategic.bear_case: Coal (~25% of earnings) faces structural ESG de-rating and price normalization; if copper's tightness is offset by a Chinese demand air-pocket, the 'trough' could deepen rather than recover, and P/B 1.62 (not below book) means there's downside room.
strategic.bull_case: Trough earnings + tight copper supply + the chronically under-credited trading arm ($2-4B EBITDA at 8-12x = mispriced) set up multi-quarter upside as the cycle inflects; potential structural moves (coal spin/exit) could re-rate the equity sharply.
strategic.falsifier: Evidence the earnings trough is deepening — LME copper breaking below ~$8,500/t alongside Chinese PMI sliding back into contraction and inventories building — would invalidate the recovery thesis and flip the sign positive.

tactical.bear_case: BTC is in a confirmed death-cross downtrend (price below both 50DMA $41.72 and 200DMA $49.23); soft RSI in a downtrend can stay soft and grind lower toward fresh lows rather than bounce. A break of $33.48 triggers stop-driven liquidation cascades.
tactical.bull_case: RSI 35 near a multi-month support shelf ($33.48), oversold crypto with no fresh negative catalyst tends to mean-revert sharply; a single positive ETF flow day or macro risk-on (VIX 18, benign credit spreads at 2.63) can produce a fast 5-10% snapback.
tactical.falsifier: A decisive daily close below $33.48 on rising volume with accelerating ETF outflows would flip this to a positive/trim score as the support thesis breaks.

positional.bear_case: This cycle may be breaking the 4-year pattern; structural ETF outflows and a multi-month death-cross downtrend suggest the drawdown extends well below current levels. My own 6.3% positional hit rate is direct evidence the 'buy the drawdown' reflex is failing now — price could see $28-30 before a durable bottom.
positional.bull_case: Spot Bitcoin at 5.6% of 52w range and 28% below 200DMA is statistically a deep-value zone; historically BTC drawdowns of this magnitude have preceded major rallies, and the structural ETF/store-of-value adoption thesis (sovereign/institutional accumulation) remains intact for the next leg.
positional.falsifier: Two consecutive weeks of sustained positive net ETF inflows alongside a reclaim of the 50DMA ($41.72) would validate the bottom and justify pushing back toward max-buy conviction; conversely a breakdown below $33.48 with outflows flips it positive.

strategic.bear_case: If the 4-year cycle is genuinely breaking and crypto enters a prolonged structural bear like 2018/2022, strategic exposure underperforms for quarters; regulatory reversal or a loss of the institutional flow narrative would impair the long-term store-of-value thesis.
strategic.bull_case: Continued spot-ETF institutionalization, potential sovereign/treasury BTC adoption, and a benign rate path (real rates easing from 2.23% TIPS) provide a multi-year structural tailwind that makes any deep drawdown a long-term accumulation opportunity.
strategic.falsifier: Clear evidence the halving cycle is intact and reasserting (new sustained uptrend with record ETF AUM) would flip this to a negative/buy strategic score; a regulatory crackdown or structural outflow trend would push it more positive.

tactical.bear_case: After a +2% pop, RSI is creeping toward 60 and a peso wobble or KO parent caution could mean-revert the ADR quickly given KOF's thin US-listed liquidity.
tactical.bull_case: Golden-cross structure intact, RSI has room to ~70, and a continued EM bid + firm peso could extend the breakout above the 50-day at $105.
tactical.falsifier: RSI spiking above ~72 with a parabolic single-day gap would flip this to a tactical trim; a break below the 50-day SMA ($105) on a peso shock would flip it to a buy.

positional.bear_case: A hawkish Fed / dovish Banxico divergence or renewed US-Mexico tariff/trade rhetoric could pressure the peso back toward 18-19, dragging the ADR regardless of solid local volumes.
positional.bull_case: Nearshoring continues to underpin Mexican consumer income and the peso; sustained MXN strength plus mid-single-digit volume growth keeps the ADR trending up off the $105 base.
positional.falsifier: MXN/USD breaking decisively above ~18.5 (peso weakness) or KOF losing the 200-day would flip positional positive (trim); a move toward 16.5 with volume acceleration would flip it more negative.

strategic.bear_case: Mexican fiscal/political risk (Sheinbaum administration, judicial reform concerns, potential US tariff friction) and a structurally weaker peso could cap the ADR for years; sugar tax expansion and PET/input cost inflation pressure margins.
strategic.bull_case: 12.5x P/E plus 3.76% growing yield plus mid-single-digit local volume growth and a resilient peso compounds to a low-teens total return — re-rating toward 15-16x as EM staples regain favor adds upside.
strategic.falsifier: A sustained peso regime break above ~19/USD on capital flight, or a structural margin reset from sugar-tax/input shocks, would flip this to a trim; conversely an EM-wide derating to single-digit P/E would deepen the buy.

Oversold can stay oversold when the cause is a fundamental margin reset, not a sentiment dip; RSI 39 has room to 30.
tactical.bull_case: A 3-sigma daily drop in a low-beta prime within a great-power-competition regime is a classic mean-reversion setup; ITA structural bid intact, and defense names snap back on capitulation flushes. RSI 39 + 22% drawdown is exactly the high-conviction tactical entry the engine flags for this archetype.
tactical.falsifier: If the -5.85% is confirmed driven by a guidance cut / large impairment with downward EPS revisions following, the tactical bounce thesis breaks and score flips toward neutral/positive.

positional.bear_case: 9.61% op margin vs 14-16% norm signals margin compression (Aerojet propellant supply chain, fixed-price program losses). If EPS revisions are rolling over, the 'compounder on sale' is actually 'compounder de-rating,' and below-both-MA + 8-day HOLD streak suggests the thesis is stalling, not bottoming.
positional.bull_case: LHX at 37.7% of 52w range with a structural multi-year DoD budget tailwind, NATO 2%+ spending, and Golden Dome/munitions demand is a rare entry into a quality prime. Backlog compounders bought this far below trend historically deliver 20d outperformance once selling exhausts.
positional.falsifier: A book-to-bill print <0.95 with negative backlog YoY, or 90d EPS revisions turning <-1%, would confirm de-rating and flip the positional score positive.

tactical.falsifier: RSI breaking below 35 on accelerating volume (genuine momentum break) would flip to a tactical buy; RSI spiking >70 into a blow-off would flip to a tactical trim.

tactical.bull_case: A -31.7% drawdown in a 46.75% op-margin AI-infrastructure leader is a rare gift; mean reversion off these tightened-band oversold readings has historically produced sharp snapbacks, especially with VIX benign at 18.4 (no systemic panic forcing further deleveraging).
tactical.falsifier: MSFT breaks decisively below the $356 52w low on expanding volume, turning the oversold setup into a fresh-low breakdown — that would flip me to a trim/avoid tactical stance.

positional.bear_case: Death cross + 31.7% drawdown reflects a real regime change in AI-infrastructure sentiment (capex digestion, OpenAI cost/governance overhang). positional.bull_case: Trading 11.6% off the low at a -20.8% trailing P/E discount to the GOOGL/META/AAPL cohort with peer-best 46.75% margins is a textbook positional accumulation zone for a franchise compounder — these dislocations in MSFT have historically resolved upward over 6-12 months.
positional.falsifier: Price reclaims the SMA50 (~$413) and the 50/200 begins to flatten/cross back — confirming the downtrend is over would justify pushing more negative; conversely a sustained break below $356 on a guide-down flips me positive.

strategic.bear_case: Forward P/E near 26x still embeds heavy AI-capex optimism; if Azure decelerates below 22% or the OpenAI partnership economics deteriorate (funding obligations, AGI/exclusivity clauses, margin drag), the multiple could compress to 20x and the 'discount' evaporates as estimates fall. tactical.bear_case: No price stabilization — below_both_death with the 50d still sliding; the drawdown extends because the de-rating is fundamental (cRPO decel / margin compression) not technical, and knife-catching at $95 sees $85 next.
tactical.bull_case: A -55% drawdown on an ~80% gross-margin, 30%+ FCF-margin franchise is rare; RSI basing near 40 with VIX a benign 18.4 sets up a sharp mean-reversion bounce on any positive cRPO or federal-contract headline.
tactical.falsifier: A decisive reclaim of the 50d SMA ($99.24) on rising volume would flip this to a clearer buy; conversely a break below the $81 52w low on heavy volume would neutralize the mean-reversion edge.

positional.bear_case: EPS revisions roll over and cRPO decelerates below 18% on the next print; the stock is in a structural de-rating from 60x to 40x forward and 'cheap vs its own history' is a value trap as the AI-workflow premium permanently compresses.
positional.bull_case: A best-in-class compounder trading at 10% of its 52w range is a generational entry; one in-line cRPO print plus a federal/agentic win re-rates it 20-30% as capital rotates back into quality SaaS.

strategic.bull_case: Forward PE has fallen to ~45-50x with cRPO still ~20%+, the cheapest the franchise has been in years; a Fed pivot lowering real rates plus Now Assist Pro Plus ASP inflection re-rates a structurally superior 30%+ FCF-margin compounder.
strategic.falsifier: A cRPO print confirming sub-18% growth with Now Assist monetization stalling would flip strategic firmly positive (trim); a real-rate decline toward 0.5% plus cRPO re-acceleration would flip it firmly negative (buy).

tactical.bear_case: The oversold reading is 'right' — a fresh Lula interference headline (forced capex, fuel subsidy, CEO/dividend pressure) is driving real, durable downside that RSI can't catch; knife-catching a state oil at -24.5% below SMA50 has repeatedly failed for me.

positional.bear_case: Sustained break below SMA200 ($15.54) would confirm a downtrend regime; if Lula extracts higher refining capex and caps fuel prices, the dividend narrative cracks and the stock re-rates structurally lower over the coming weeks.

strategic.falsifier: A formal dividend-policy cut or a mandated large low-return capex program flips this clearly positive; conversely an oil breakout above $95 with reaffirmed dividend policy would flip me negative/buy.

tactical.bear_case: Price is below both the 50d ($476.88) and 200d ($525.82) in a death-cross configuration with no momentum turn yet; deep drawdowns can extend, and a name 30% off its high in a death cross has no technical floor confirmed. RSI 46 is not oversold, so there's no mean-reversion spring loaded.
tactical.bull_case: A -28% drawdown in a quality compounder near a cycle trough is exactly the asymmetric tactical entry; XBI up ~1% and TMO outperforming DHR today hint biotech sympathy bid returning, which historically leads TMO. Tactical BUY signals hit 77.8% over fwd 5d historically.
tactical.falsifier: TMO breaks decisively below the 52w low of $385.46 on rising volume with XBI rolling over, signaling the drawdown is fundamental deterioration not a trough — would flip tactical positive (trim/avoid).

positional.bear_case: Death cross with price below both MAs reflects a real multi-quarter downtrend; bioprocessing destocking and NIH funding uncertainty could keep organic growth at ~1% longer than expected, and there's no confirmed cycle inflection in the print yet — the 'trough' could be a longer plateau, leaving positioning stuck.
positional.bull_case: This is the late-trough setup the archetype targets — quality compounder 28% off highs with bioprocessing destocking ending and biotech funding thawing.
