If GLP-1 disruption and biotech capital discipline persist, 'normalized' growth may settle below the historical mid-single-digit algorithm, justifying a permanently lower multiple rather than a re-rate.
strategic.bull_case: Picks-and-shovels monopoly with pricing power, PPD CRO scale, and Wave 4 AI life-sciences optionality (bioproduction for AI-discovered biologics) buying near a cyclical earnings trough at a below-history multiple. As the cycle confirms toward 5%+ organic and margins recover off 17.5%, both EPS and the multiple expand simultaneously — the most powerful strategic return profile.
strategic.falsifier: Forward P/E re-rating above ~28x WITHOUT growth re-acceleration (multiple expansion on hope), OR confirmed simultaneous China capex collapse + sustained NIH cuts impairing the structural growth algorithm — either would flip strategic toward trim.

tactical.bear_case: Spot freight rates (WCI/SCFI) continue softening as massive newbuild capacity floods the market in 2026; a break of the $11.47 200-day MA on volume opens further downside, and the 50-day is already declining — momentum is rolling over.

tactical.bear_case: Stock is 19.4% above SMA50 and has run hard into earnings; any mean-reversion pullback to the 50-day would be a ~16% drawdown, and stretched names give back fast when WFE sentiment wobbles.

tactical.bear_case: ENB just stalled at the 50-day after a run; if the 10Y backs up toward 4.7% on hot inflation data, rate-sensitive midstream could see a sympathetic 2-3% pullback that a tactical short anticipates.

tactical.bull_case: With WTI firm at $84.65 and the move not oil-driven, a 27% RSI with no fundamental deterioration is a clean snap-back setup; ADR could recover 3-5% in days once the BRL stabilizes.
tactical.falsifier: A confirmed government action (forced fuel price freeze, special-dividend cut, or CEO replacement) hitting the tape would flip this from oversold-buy to falling-knife sell.

positional.bear_case: The 24.5% gap below SMA50 reflects a real de-rating on Brazil fiscal/political risk; trend-followers and EM outflows can keep PBR.A pinned below its MAs for months regardless of oil.
positional.bull_case: Price above SMA200 ($15.6 vs... actually below) — pre-salt breakeven ~$35 means $84 oil throws off enormous FCF supporting the 9.4% yield; positional buyers get paid to wait for mean reversion to SMA50.
positional.falsifier: A reclaim of SMA50 ($19.6) on volume would confirm trend repair (more bullish), while a sustained break below SMA200/$15.6 would confirm trend continuation lower and flip the sign positive.

strategic.bear_case: 5x peak PE + Lula's track record of forcing refining capex and capping fuel prices means dividend payout ratio gets squeezed exactly as oil rolls over — the 9.4% yield is a value trap if both earnings and distributions compress.
strategic.bull_case: Pre-salt is among the lowest-cost barrels globally (~$35 breakeven); at $84 WTI Petrobras gushes FCF, and even a 'normalized' payout leaves a high-single-digit yield with optionality on production growth — a long-term EM income compounder.
strategic.falsifier: A formal change in dividend policy (statutory payout reduction) or government-mandated capex diversion into non-core assets would flip this firmly positive; conversely a credible commitment to capital discipline + buybacks would flip it negative.

tactical.bear_case: If Red Sea/Suez reopening accelerates, effective capacity surges and spot rates collapse fast — shipping rates move violently and a 10-15% gap-down is plausible on any week of WCI prints, pushing the tactical short-term lower.
tactical.bull_case: A fresh Middle East escalation or new Houthi attacks would re-extend Cape of Good Hope routing, spiking rates and the stock 10%+ in days given operating leverage; RSI sub-50 leaves room to run.
tactical.falsifier: A decisive break above the 50-day with RSI clearing 60 on rising volume (or a sharp WCI spot rate spike) would flip this to a tactical buy (negative).

positional.bear_case: Record orderbook deliveries in 2026-27 structurally oversupply container capacity; as Red Sea normalizes the rate cliff arrives and the golden cross unwinds — positional should be more positive (trim).
positional.bull_case: Maersk's logistics segment is being priced near zero vs DSV/K+N peers (~15-20x EV/EBITDA); any margin recovery there plus sustained buybacks/dividend (~3% yield) supports the share base and the uptrend continues — argues for negative score.
positional.falsifier: A sustained reversal below the 200-day SMA ($11.49) on deteriorating freight rates would flip this firmly positive; conversely a confirmed freight rate uptrend with rising volumes would flip it negative.

strategic.bear_case: Structural overcapacity from the largest orderbook in history depresses freight rates for years; 0.70 P/B can stay cheap or get cheaper if ROIC craters below cost of capital — book value itself erodes via vessel impairments, making the 'discount' a value trap.
strategic.bull_case: Net-cash balance sheet funds aggressive buybacks at sub-book prices (highly accretive), logistics turnaround re-rates the stub, and any trade-volume recovery (CPB World Trade Monitor stabilizing) on a P/B 0.70 base gives meaningful upside — argues for a stronger negative score.
strategic.falsifier: A break of the freight rate floor that pushes Maersk back toward operating losses / negative FCF, or book-value-destroying impairments, would flip this strategic score positive.

tactical.bear_case: Two back-to-back >3.5% up days into a 58x trailing multiple is the kind of momentum chase that mean-reverts; a sector cool-off or a single soft data point could give back the rally quickly given how far above SMA50 it sits.
tactical.bull_case: RSI at 55.89 has plenty of room before overbought; if AI capex headlines (TSMC/NVDA ecosystem) keep flowing, ASML can continue trending higher with no technical exhaustion signal, making any trim premature.
tactical.falsifier: RSI breaking above 70 with the stock extending another 5%+ in days would flip this to a clearer trim; conversely RSI sliding back under 45 on a pullback would flip to a buy lean.

positional.bear_case: 13.6% above SMA50 is a stretched positional setup; pullbacks to the 50-day are common for ASML and would mean ~12% downside before the trend even threatens, arguing for trimming into strength.
positional.bull_case: Persistent above-MA trending is exactly how monopoly compounders behave; the gap above SMA50 reflects a genuine re-rating on improving order trajectory, and fading it has been a losing trade all year.
positional.falsifier: A close below the SMA50 ($1607) on rising volume would flip this positive (trim/avoid); a successful shallow pullback that holds the 20-day and resumes would keep it near neutral-to-buy.

strategic.bear_case: If forward P/E is also creeping toward/above 45x on the rally, the 'extreme valuation' trim trigger is genuinely met; combined with China revenue exposure to tightening export controls and any sign of TSMC/Samsung/Intel pacing capex, the secular premium could compress meaningfully from here.
strategic.bull_case: ASML is the irreplaceable bottleneck for AI compute scaling; High-NA EUV at $350M+/tool and a multi-year backlog mean earnings are set to inflate, collapsing the forward multiple — today's 'extreme' trailing P/E is a backward-looking artifact and the stock remains a core long-term compounder to add on weakness.
strategic.falsifier: Forward P/E confirmed under 25x (via a backlog/order surge or earnings beat) flips this to a buy; conversely confirmation of forward P/E >45x AND book-to-bill <1.0 with broad capex cuts would push the score sharply more positive.

tactical.bear_case: Stock is near 52w highs with a thin ~60bps yield cushion vs bonds; if the 10Y reverses higher on a hot PCE print or the Fed's hawkish tilt, yield names like ENB de-rate first, pulling price back toward the 200DMA.
tactical.bull_case: The 10Y is falling (oil at four-month lows, flight-to-safety bid), which directly supports bond proxies; ENB could melt up toward $58.45 if rate-cut odds re-emerge or the Iran de-escalation sustains the risk-off bid into Treasuries.
tactical.falsifier: A decisive break and hold above the $58.45 52w high on rising volume, or the 10Y dropping below ~4.0%, would flip this to a tactical buy as the yield spread re-widens.

positional.bear_case: With a sub-70bps spread to the 10Y, near 52w highs, and only ~3% dividend growth, the income+growth math offers little forward edge; a hawkish Fed and 5.0x leverage cap upside, making ENB a relative funding source vs cheaper book names over the next 20 days.
positional.bull_case: Falling long rates plus a reaffirmed $40B secured backlog and record Mainline volumes (3.2 MMbpd, apportioned all year) give ENB durable, contracted cash-flow growth; if rates keep falling, the bond-proxy bid drives outperformance and the spread argument inverts.
positional.falsifier: The yield spread vs the 10Y widening back above ~150bps (via a price pullback or a sharp drop in the 10Y) would flip this to a buy, as would a backlog/guidance raise above the current 5% CAGR.

strategic.bear_case: In a structurally higher-for-longer / hawkish-Fed rate regime, a 5%-growth bond proxy at a ~60bps spread is permanently less attractive; CAD/USD FX drag and top-of-range 5.0x leverage cap the multiple, so ENB lags higher-growth book names over multi-year horizons.
strategic.bull_case: Greg Ebel calls this the best energy-infrastructure growth environment in 10-15 years; LNG buildout and data-center gas demand could push the backlog and CAGR above 5%, compounding a safe, growing 5% yield into durable total returns that justify the premium.
strategic.falsifier: A guidance/backlog upgrade lifting the through-2030 CAGR above 5%, or a structural decline in long rates restoring a 150bps+ yield spread, would flip strategic to a buy.

tactical.bear_case: The -3.7% drop on a day copper miners rose signals real GLNCY-specific selling (coal/ESG overhang, fund liquidation, or trading-arm concern) that momentum can extend; RSI 33 is oversold but not capitulation (sub-30), so there's room lower toward the 200-day.
tactical.bull_case: Thin-ADR mechanical selloff against a backdrop of surging copper and tight HY spreads sets up a sharp catch-up snapback; oversold RSI plus 5pp COPX outperformance is a textbook mean-reversion entry.
tactical.falsifier: A close below the 200-day SMA ($12.36) on rising volume would flip this from oversold-bounce setup to confirmed breakdown and turn the tactical score positive.

positional.bear_case: GLNCY lags COPX for a structural reason — its coal/ESG discount and lumpy trading-arm earnings — meaning the 'catch-up' may never arrive and the spread reflects deserved derating rather than a temporary gap.
positional.bull_case: Copper's strength (LME near multi-month highs, tight inventories, China stimulus restocking) plus GLNCY's below-replacement asset value and underpriced trading arm means the laggard re-rates violently once generalist flows rotate into diversified miners.
positional.falsifier: COPX rolling over (copper price breaking down on weak China PMI/property data) while GLNCY continues to lag would remove the catch-up driver and flip the positional score positive.

strategic.bear_case: Coal remains ~25% of EBITDA and faces secular ESG-driven multiple compression and eventual demand decline; if China's property-led metals demand stays structurally weaker, the supercycle thesis stalls and GLNCY's discount persists for years.
strategic.bull_case: Structural copper supply deficit (grid electrification + AI/datacenter power + EV) meets GLNCY's tier-one copper assets and underappreciated trading franchise; buybacks and a re-rate toward replacement value drive multi-year outperformance.
strategic.falsifier: Evidence the copper deficit thesis is breaking — sustained copper price decline plus rising LME inventories plus deteriorating Chinese industrial data — would invalidate the strategic buy and turn the score positive.

tactical.bear_case: BTC is in a structural downtrend, RSI 31 is not deeply oversold (no capitulation <30), and tactical bounces in death-cross regimes routinely fail and roll over to new lows — the 52w low at $33.48 is about to break, triggering stop cascades and forced ETF outflows.
tactical.bull_case: BTC is coiled at multi-month support with RSI oversold; a single positive flow day or macro relief (softer CPI, rate-cut repricing) detonates a sharp short-cover rally given my 100% fwd_5d tactical hit rate here.
tactical.falsifier: A decisive daily close below $33.48 (the 52w low) on rising volume would flip this from oversold-bounce to breakdown, turning the tactical score positive.

positional.bear_case: 9 straight days of BUY signals all faded; the 52w low is right here and about to give way. If ETF flows have structurally turned net-redemptive and this cycle breaks the 4-year pattern, 'cheap vs 200DMA' is meaningless — BTC can fall another 30-40% as the 200DMA itself keeps declining. The calibration screams the positional model is generating noise (POOR 23.6%).
positional.bull_case: Spot Bitcoin ETFs structurally cap how deep drawdowns go versus prior cycles; -30% below 200DMA with a holding base of sovereign/institutional buyers is precisely the accumulation zone that delivers outsized fwd_20d returns. One flow inflection reverses the whole tape violently.
positional.falsifier: A sustained break below $33.48 with accelerating net ETF outflows (multi-day) would confirm the drawdown is structural, not a dip, and push this score toward neutral or positive.

strategic.bear_case: If this cycle is genuinely breaking the 4-year pattern — ETF flows saturating, no new marginal buyer cohort — then BTC enters a prolonged bear and the strategic store-of-value narrative stalls for years, justifying a positive (trim) tilt.
strategic.bull_case: Structural ETF/sovereign adoption is a multi-year secular driver; a regulatory unlock (401k inclusion, more nation-state buyers) or a Fed easing cycle could re-rate BTC well above prior highs, making any strategic trim a mistake.
strategic.falsifier: Evidence of durable net ETF outflows over a multi-month span plus LTH capitulation would signal the secular adoption thesis is stalling and flip this to a positive/trim score.

tactical.bear_case: Stock sits near the top of its range after a strong run; if MXN reverses toward the 17.5–17.6 forecast band on a hawkish Fed / narrowing rate differential, the ADR gives back gains quickly with no near-term earnings catalyst until Feb 2026.
tactical.bull_case: Peso carry strength persists (Banxico pausing cuts, narrowing differential favoring MXN), DXY soft near 98, and momentum/MA structure remains a 'buy' with RSI not yet overbought — the ADR can grind higher into the July dividend ex-date.
tactical.falsifier: A decisive USD/MXN break above 17.60 with KOF rolling below its 50-day MA on rising volume would flip this from mild-trim toward buy-the-dip.

positional.bear_case: Mexico (the most profitable market) volume is contracting on the excise tax, IT/restructuring costs pressure margins all year, financing costs are rising, and the ADR is in the upper third of its range at full multiple — limited multiple expansion room, with risk of de-rating if SA momentum cools or peso weakens.
positional.bull_case: Gross margin is expanding on benign PET/sweetener costs and FX, SA is delivering record Q1 volumes with digital (Juntos+) gains, FIFA World Cup 2026 is a volume catalyst, and a strong/stable peso translates directly into higher ADR earnings — a fairly-valued compounder can keep compounding with the dividend.
positional.falsifier: Q2/Q3 showing Mexico volumes inflecting positive AND consolidated operating income returning to growth (IT costs rolling off) would flip this to a buy tilt; conversely a peso break to 18+ would deepen the trim.

strategic.bear_case: Structural Mexican excise-tax pressure on its most profitable market, secular soft-drink/health headwinds, EM-currency translation risk (peso could revisit 18–20 in a US-tariff/risk-off scenario), and limited organic volume growth (2026 guided flattish) cap the long-term compounding rate relative to global staples peers.
strategic.bull_case: Wide moat + largest global bottler scale + underpenetrated LatAm consumption + digital distribution + a stable/strong peso regime and falling input costs support double-digit currency-neutral revenue growth and reliable dividend compounding — exactly the EM dividend-growth profile the portfolio wants, at a reasonable 16.7x.
strategic.falsifier: A sustained peso regime shift to 19–20 MXN/USD, or a structural acceleration of Mexico volume declines (excise tax proving permanently demand-destructive), would flip the strategic sign to positive (trim).

tactical.bear_case: LHX is below both MAs in a death cross with a -6.6% gap below SMA50; deep drawdowns in falling-knife setups can extend further, and the op margin compression (9.61%) signals the drawdown reflects fundamentals, not just noise. A -23% drawdown can become -30%.
tactical.bull_case: A defense prime at 35.5% of its 52w range with great-power-competition tailwinds intact is rarely a value trap; any program win or budget headline snaps a -23% gap shut fast, and today's +1.64% bounce off support hints accumulation.
tactical.falsifier: A close below the 52w low of $243.84 with accelerating volume would flip this to a momentum-breakdown SELL.

positional.bear_case: Op margin at 9.61% (vs 14-16% norm) with EPS revisions unknown could mean the death cross and 35% range position are correctly pricing deteriorating profitability, not a buying opportunity — backlog conversion and Aerojet integration may be margin-dilutive longer than expected.
positional.bull_case: Smallest of the Big 5 primes at a deep discount with the strongest secular DoD tailwind in a decade; if B/B is still >1.0 and backlog is expanding, this -23% drawdown is a textbook compounder-on-sale entry that re-rates toward cohort.
positional.falsifier: A book-to-bill print below 0.95 with negative backlog YoY, or a guide-down on margins, would flip this positive (trim).

strategic.bear_case: If the 9.61% op margin reflects structural cost/supply-chain pressure (titanium, energetics, Aerojet integration drag) rather than transient mix, then trailing 31x is not cheap on a normalized basis and the strategic re-rating thesis is impaired.
strategic.bull_case: Forward P/E is meaningfully below trailing 31x; with backlog providing multi-year revenue visibility and DoD budget in real expansion, normalized margins recovering to 14%+ make today's valuation attractive on a 3-year view.
strategic.falsifier: Confirmation that op margins are structurally re-rating toward mid-teens (or a major multi-billion program award) would flip this to a strategic buy; conversely a sequester/CR shock would deepen the trim.

tactical.bear_case: Stock is within 2% of all-time high after an 18% YTD run; a momentum/quality-factor rotation (QUAL underperforming SPY) or any AI-capex wobble could trigger mean-reversion in an extended, low-beta name that has little near-term catalyst until July 23 earnings.
tactical.bull_case: Defensive low-beta compounder with bullish chart structure (high-base consolidation just under highs); helium pricing inflecting higher and continued AI/electronics order flow could drive a clean breakout above $526 with no overhead supply.
tactical.falsifier: A decisive breakout and close above the $526 all-time high on strong volume would flip the tactical read to a buy/momentum-continuation bias (negative score).

positional.bear_case: Underlying volume growth is anemic (Q1 underlying sales +3%, only +1% volume; EPS driven by price and buybacks, not demand); EMEA volumes are declining and FX (1% tailwind) flattered the quarter. If the DXY firms or pricing power fades, the price-led EPS algorithm slows and the still-elevated 33x multiple compresses toward the 25.9x fair ratio.
positional.bull_case: Peer premium vs APD has fully compressed to in-line while LIN retains superior margins (30% vs APD 24%) and ROC; guidance was raised, helium pricing is inflecting, and electronics backlog is building — earnings revisions are skewing up, supporting multiple maintenance and continued relative outperformance.
positional.falsifier: A re-widening of LIN's P/E premium back above ~10% over APD, or a downward FY EPS guidance revision at the Q2 print, would flip the positional read to trim (positive score).

strategic.bear_case: At ~33x vs a 25.9x fair ratio and 27.8x industry, multiple risk dominates the long-run return: even with 7-9% EPS growth, a de-rating to high-20s P/E would erase years of compounding. Green-hydrogen economics remain unproven and IRA 45V credit uncertainty could strand the decarbonization growth narrative.
strategic.bull_case: Oligopoly pricing power plus three simultaneous secular demand vectors (AI fabs, commercial space, helium scarcity) underwrite durable mid-to-high-single-digit EPS compounding with expanding margins; a fortress balance sheet in tight-credit conditions lets LIN buy back stock and fund growth, justifying a sustained premium multiple.
strategic.falsifier: Evidence the oligopoly's pricing power is breaking (sustained negative like-for-like pricing) or a structural loss of electronics/space share to APD or Air Liquide would flip the strategic case to trim (positive score).

tactical.bear_case: RSI can stay oversold in a genuine regime break; MSFT below both MAs with a death-cross structure means trend followers are sellers, and 10Y at 4.5% with real rates 2.29% pressures long-duration mega-cap multiples. A bounce can fail and re-test lower.
tactical.bull_case: Sub-31 RSI plus 20% below 200DMA on the highest-quality AI-infra balance sheet is a high-probability snapback; any reassuring Azure/Copilot headline or broad market relief rally lifts MSFT fastest given its low beta and quality bid.
tactical.falsifier: RSI breaking back above 50 with price reclaiming SMA50 (~$412) would flip this from oversold-buy to neutral/trend-resumption; conversely a close that holds RSI <25 on heavy volume would deepen the buy.

positional.bear_case: Below both MAs with a death cross during a 4.5% 10Y / 2.29% real-rate regime can reflect a legitimate de-rating of AI-infra capex names; if the drawdown is driven by Azure deceleration fears or capex-ROI skepticism, the dip keeps deepening and mechanical mean-reversion fails — exactly what the POOR positional hit rate warns.
positional.bull_case: A 20% drawdown on a compounder of MSFT's quality is historically a rare entry; quality factor flows and buyback support provide a floor, and any Azure CC stabilization at high-20s% re-rates the stock quickly back toward the MAs.
positional.falsifier: Price reclaiming and holding above SMA50 (~$412) would neutralize the stretched-down thesis; conversely confirmation of Azure CC decel below 22% on the next print would convert the technical dip into a fundamental down-leg and kill the buy.

strategic.bear_case: Restrictive real rates persist, hyperscaler capex arms race continues with MSFT committing while ROI on AI infrastructure remains unproven; OpenAI partnership renegotiation/AGI-clause risk or a Copilot monetization air-pocket could reset secular expectations and compress the multiple further.
strategic.bull_case: Forward P/E now compressed toward the low end of the 3Y band with Azure CC intact, OpenAI distribution moat plus Copilot enterprise traction, and ~10% buyback yield mean a 5-year compounder at a discount entry; rate relief is an upside option on top.
strategic.falsifier: Azure constant-currency growth re-accelerating above 30% with Copilot seat growth >50% YoY would push strategic decisively more negative (stronger buy); a confirmed OpenAI partnership unwind or Azure decel below 22% would flip the sign positive.

tactical.bear_case: Oversold can stay oversold: stock has been in a relentless downtrend, technicals read Strong Sell on MA stack, and AI-agent disruption headlines (Anthropic, MSFT Copilot) keep hitting the sector. A break of $91.76 trend floor / $84.78 support opens another leg down with no earnings cushion until July 22.
tactical.bull_case: RSI ~26 with a fresh pivot-bottom signal and a 55% drawdown sets up a sharp short-covering bounce (5.9% short interest); software has already shown oversold-rally episodes in late May (NOW led a software rebound). Any positive read-through from peer prints or analyst upgrades (Benchmark just raised PT to $130) can snap it 5-8% quickly.
tactical.falsifier: A decisive close below $84.78 horizontal support on rising volume (confirming trend continuation) would flip this to neutral/positive; conversely a reclaim of the 50-day (~$104) would deepen the BUY.

positional.bear_case: The de-rate is not noise — cRPO guided to decelerate to 19.5% cc (from 21%), the exact 'growth normalizing, not re-accelerating' tell that justifies a lower multiple permanently. If agentic AI compresses seat-based pricing, NOW's ~22x forward PE is not cheap but fair-to-rich for a business decelerating toward high-teens growth. Hawkish Fed + Middle East deal-timing headwinds + Armis margin drag give bears more time.
positional.bull_case: A best-in-class ~31.5% op-margin, ~35% FCF-margin compounder growing subscription ~20% at a sub-6x EV/NTM revenue and ~22x forward PE is a generational valuation for this franchise; analyst targets cluster $130-142 (40-50% upside) with a Strong Buy consensus. cRPO beat its own guide by 100bps and AI ACV is triple-digit — a single in-line July print could re-rate the multiple violently given how compressed it is.
positional.falsifier: Q2 (July 22) cRPO printing below ~18% cc (thesis air-pocket) or a guide-down would flip the sign to positive; a cRPO re-acceleration above ~21% cc would push toward STRONG_BUY.

strategic.bear_case: If AI agents commoditize the workflow layer — buyers orchestrating directly via Anthropic/OpenAI or MSFT's distribution scale — NOW's seat-and-module model structurally erodes and 20% growth fades to low-teens, justifying a permanently lower multiple. The market is repricing the entire SaaS model ($2T erased); NOW is squarely in the crosshairs and the narrative may not reverse for years.
strategic.bull_case: ServiceNow's platform-substrate positioning is the most defensible in enterprise SaaS — agents need governance, data fabric and orchestration, which is exactly what the Now Platform provides. If AI ACV (already triple-digit growth) becomes the next growth engine, NOW re-accelerates above 20% and re-rates from a crisis multiple back toward compounder norms, delivering multi-year outperformance from today's reset price.
strategic.falsifier: Two-plus consecutive quarters of cRPO deceleration toward/below mid-teens, or evidence of net AI-driven seat cannibalization in disclosed metrics, would flip the sign to positive; sustained AI-ACV-led re-acceleration would deepen the BUY.

tactical.bear_case: Oil downtrend is intact, not exhausted — Brent just entered contango for the first time since the conflict began, money managers are cutting longs, and Iraq is threatening to leave OPEC over quotas, raising supply-flood risk. A falling-knife oil tape drags PBR.A lower over the next 5 days regardless of valuation.
tactical.bull_case: After a ~40% oil drop and PBR.A near 52-week lows with an ~11% yield, any stabilization in crude (Cushing at multi-decade-low inventories, Saudi defending an ~$80 floor) triggers a sharp oversold bounce in a high-beta name.
tactical.falsifier: Two consecutive up-days in Brent with the prompt spread returning to backwardation would flip this to a cleaner positive-momentum buy; conversely a decisive Brent break below $65 would push the score positive (avoid).

positional.bear_case: JPM models Brent ~$60 for 2026 and just cut PBR's target to $23; at $60 oil plus a weakening real (Selic cuts narrowing the rate differential, dollar broadly strong), the fat dividend gets cut and the 'cheap' multiple re-rates lower as troughing earnings catch down.
positional.bull_case: Classic cyclical bottom: high trailing P/E reflects trough earnings, stock at 52-week lows, ~11% yield, political risk quiet, and Saudi defending an ~$80 floor — a snapback in oil over the next 1-3 months drives outsized recovery off a washed-out base.
positional.falsifier: A renewed Lula dividend block / CEO ouster, or a sustained Brent move under $60, would flip this positive (trim); a confirmed oil-price floor with stable BRL would deepen the buy.

strategic.bear_case: Lula's documented willingness to block dividends and fire the CEO over payouts is a permanent governance discount; with a 2027 election cycle approaching, fuel-price populism and capex-for-jobs pressure rises, structurally capping total return and risking the dividend that is the entire thesis.
strategic.bull_case: Pre-salt growth + best-in-class low-cost barrels + double-digit yield means that even at $60 Brent, Petrobras generates large free cash flow; at a ~1.1x book and ~5x P/E with state risk already heavily priced, the long-run risk/reward skews to the upside for income-plus-optionality.
strategic.falsifier: A formal change to the market-based fuel pricing bylaw (return to subsidized parity) or a structural dividend-policy cut would flip this positive; durable non-interference plus $70+ oil would deepen the buy.

tactical.bear_case: The +4.9% June 24 jump already captured much of the near-term thaw; TMO has overhead supply between here and $520-540, and a single fewer-selling-day optics risk into the late-July Q2 print could cap upside. After a sharp one-day move, mean-reversion/profit-taking is the base case over 5 days.
tactical.bull_case: XBI near 52w highs with sustained M&A ($106B YTD deals) is dragging healthcare sentiment up; TMO is a high-beta quality proxy for the thaw and could continue grinding toward $510+ as the bid broadens, especially with BIO International 2026 visibility and Clario momentum.
tactical.falsifier: XBI rolls over >5% from its ~$148 high on a risk-off healthcare tape, or TMO fails to hold above its breakout (~$470-475) and closes back below the 50-day, flipping near-term momentum negative.

positional.bear_case: The cycle inflection is 'real but young' — only 1-2 clean quarters of double-digit consumable orders against a memory of a two-year downcycle. If Q2 organic lands below ~3% (the days/phasing tailwind fails to materialize), the discount is justified and the stock fades. China life-science capex and US academic/government (NIH) headwinds persist and hit Analytical Instruments (-2% organic) and Specialty Diagnostics (-3% organic).
positional.bull_case: TMO is the quality laggard to a fully-thawed XBI; as Q2 confirms ~3% organic and 2H ramp, the NTM EV/EBITDA discount becomes hard to justify and the multiple re-rates from trough toward historical. Clario + Solventum filtration + US reshoring CDMO demand provide incremental organic upside not yet in the price; 10% dividend hike and $3B buyback signal management confidence.
positional.falsifier: Q2 2026 organic growth prints below ~2% (confirming the trough is not lifting) OR XBI breaks down >10% from highs into a frozen regime, removing the booking-lead tailwind — either would flip the positional sign.

strategic.bear_case: $43.2B total debt post-Clario plus aggressive $3B/quarter buybacks raise financial-strength concerns (GF Financial Strength 5/10). Structural overhangs — GLP-1 disruption to diagnostics, China policy/localization eroding share, NIH/academic funding cuts under the current US administration, and FDA turmoil — could cap the long-run organic algorithm below the historical mid-single-digit+ pace. M&A-driven growth (Clario, Solventum) masks soft organic.
strategic.bull_case: A multi-year bioprocessing up-cycle plus reshoring of US pharma manufacturing creates a durable demand wave for TMO's consumables, CDMO and bioprocessing tools; as organic re-accelerates to 4-6%, the stock re-rates back toward a 25-30x multiple, compounding off a trough base — a setup that doesn't recur often for a franchise of this quality.
strategic.falsifier: Evidence the structural grower is permanently impaired — e.g., two-plus consecutive quarters of sub-trend bioprocessing orders across the peer group (Repligen/Danaher/Sartorius decelerating), or a step-change down in China/NIH demand that resets the long-run organic algorithm below ~3%.

tactical.bear_case: If a Red Sea normalization (ceasefire/Suez reopening) headline hits, freight rates collapse fast as ~10% of effective capacity returns — Maersk could gap down 5-8% on any de-escalation news, and the chart's golden cross would reverse quickly.
tactical.bull_case: Renewed Red Sea/Houthi escalation or a new tariff-driven front-loading surge could spike spot rates again, and with price riding above both MAs the stock has momentum to extend; container equities re-rate violently on rate spikes.
tactical.falsifier: A sustained break of the 50-day SMA ($12.13) on rising volume alongside falling WCI prints would flip this tactical read negative (toward trim).

positional.bear_case: Massive newbuild deliveries through 2026 swamp demand; spot rates mean-revert toward cash-cost, EBITDA guidance gets cut, and the golden cross unwinds as the positional thesis (rate normalization) plays out against the bulls.
positional.bull_case: P/B at 0.70 (below replacement value of the fleet) plus aggressive buybacks and dividend create a floor; if rates hold elevated via Red Sea disruption into 2026, consensus EBITDA upgrades drive the stock higher over the coming weeks.
positional.falsifier: A guidance cut from Maersk citing oversupply, or WCI breaking below ~$1,800/FEU, would flip the positional sign toward trim.

strategic.bear_case: Shipping is structurally low-ROIC; below-book is the market correctly pricing a chronic value-destroyer entering a supply-glut downcycle. The logistics integration (LF Logistics, Hamburg Süd) has underwhelmed, and normalized mid-cycle earnings imply the stock is fairly valued, not cheap.
strategic.bull_case: 0.70x book on a fleet whose replacement cost has risen with steel/labor inflation means downside is protected, while buybacks shrink the share count meaningfully; any structurally higher rate floor (decarbonization slow-steaming, Suez detours becoming semi-permanent) re-rates the whole complex.
strategic.falsifier: A move of P/B sustainably above ~1.0 alongside a collapse to single-digit trailing P/E (signaling peak earnings) would flip strategic to trim.

tactical.bear_case: Stock is 107.6% of 52w high and 11.4% above SMA50; a modest pullback toward the rising 50-day ($1614) is natural after a strong run, and a -2.4% day could signal momentum fatigue.
tactical.bull_case: Mid-range RSI with no overbought signal means there's room to run; a single down day on no specific news is noise for a name with 2-3yr backlog visibility — dips get bought.
tactical.falsifier: A sustained break below SMA50 ($1614) on heavy volume, or RSI collapsing below 40, would flip this to a buy-the-dip negative score.

positional.bear_case: Price sits ~39% above the 200-day; that gap is wide and any AI-capex disappointment could trigger a sharp mean-reversion given how much good news is priced in.
positional.bull_case: Persistent new highs with mid-range RSI reflect durable institutional accumulation into the High-NA ramp; the trend is intact and pullbacks have been shallow and bought.
positional.falsifier: A drawdown exceeding 15% from highs (toward ~$1455) would flip positional decisively to buy bias per archetype guidance.

strategic.bear_case: If the China DUV backlog rolls off under tightening export controls and leading-edge customers stretch fab timelines, 2026-27 EPS estimates de-rate and a 58x trailing multiple compresses hard.
strategic.bull_case: AI compute demand pulls leading-edge capex forward; High-NA at $350M+/tool plus accelerating TSMC revenue drives upward EPS revisions, making today's forward multiple cheap in hindsight for a monopoly compounding 15%+.
strategic.falsifier: Forward P/E falling below ~25x on a >15% drawdown, OR backlog/book-to-bill accelerating with TSMC capex raised, would flip strategic to clear buy.

tactical.bear_case: Price sits just above the 50-day ($55.04) after a recent grind higher; a stall here with no momentum could give back the last percent if the 10Y backs up toward 4.5%+.
tactical.bull_case: Constructive golden-cross structure and a flat tape with low VIX means dip-buyers of the 4.9% yield can step in on any minor weakness, providing a floor.
tactical.falsifier: An RSI move outside the 35-65 band or a >40bp single-session 10Y move would create a real tactical setup and flip this off zero.

positional.bear_case: 46bps yield spread is near the tightest in ENB's 5-year range; if the BoC/Fed hold higher-for-longer, a 4.9% equity yield with bond-like growth offers poor relative value and the stock can drift sideways while bonds pay you to wait — classic opportunity-cost trim.
positional.bull_case: Rate-cut cycle resumption (BoC already cutting) compresses the discount rate for long-duration income; with LNG Canada volumes ramping and contracted fee-based EBITDA growing ~5%, the yield spread can normalize via the 10Y falling rather than ENB falling, making today's 'rich' look cheap in hindsight.
positional.falsifier: A 10Y rally toward ~4.0% (pushing spread back above ~85bps) combined with sustained dividend coverage would flip this to a buy; conversely a dividend-coverage scare would push it harder to trim.

strategic.bear_case: At 24.9x P/E and a sub-150bps yield spread, ENB offers no margin of safety; long-duration restrictive real rates (TIPS 2.23%) cap multiple expansion, and a high payout ratio limits reinvestment, so total return likely lags the broader book over the next year.
strategic.bull_case: ENB's pivot toward regulated US gas utilities plus LNG Canada and Gulf Coast export buildout structurally de-risks and grows the fee base; in a falling-real-rate environment this becomes a premium bond-proxy and re-rates higher while compounding the dividend.
strategic.falsifier: Real rates (TIPS) falling toward ~1.5% or a yield spread re-widening above ~150bps would remove the valuation drag and flip the sign toward accumulate; a dividend cut or major pipeline-permitting failure would push it further to trim.

tactical.bear_case: Price remains below the 50-day MA, meaning the intermediate trend is still down. If LME copper rolls over on soft Chinese property/PMI data, GLNCY can quickly retest the lows and the oversold bounce fails. Coal pricing weakness and ESG-driven multiple compression remain overhangs that cap rallies.
tactical.bull_case: RSI in mid-30s with a confirmed up-day and price holding well above the 200-day is a classic mean-reversion entry. With copper near multi-year highs and the trading arm providing a floor, dips in a structurally tight copper market get bought quickly.
tactical.falsifier: A decisive break below the 200-day ($12.39) on rising volume, or LME copper breaking down through key support, would flip this to a trim/avoid stance.

positional.bear_case: China remains the marginal copper and zinc consumer; persistent property-sector weakness and weak Chinese PMI could stall the copper rally. Coal (25% of mix) faces both ESG divestment pressure and softening thermal prices. The below-50-day position means momentum has not yet turned.
positional.bull_case: Copper's secular demand story (electrification, grid, AI datacenter power) plus a tight HY credit backdrop and the trading arm's earnings floor create asymmetric upside. If the stock reclaims the 50-day, momentum buyers re-engage and the diversification premium expands.
positional.falsifier: GLNCY/COPX spread turning sharply negative (GLNCY lagging copper) combined with HY OAS widening past ~3.5bps would signal demand deterioration and flip the score positive.

strategic.bear_case: P/B 1.61 is NOT below replacement cost, so this is not a no-brainer asset-value buy. If the trough is deepening rather than recovering — driven by a China hard-landing, prolonged coal weakness, and ESG capital flight from the coal franchise — the high P/E reflects genuine earnings impairment, not a cyclical low to be bought.
strategic.bull_case: Copper structural deficits widen as supply additions disappoint and electrification/datacenter demand accelerates; coal cash flows remain a cash cow funding buybacks. The trading arm's trough-floor earnings plus a recovering mining cycle produce sharp EBITDA upside, and the trough P/E will collapse as earnings normalize — a textbook cyclical re-rate.
strategic.falsifier: Evidence the earnings trough is deepening — LME copper breaking down sustainably, Chinese industrial demand collapsing, and management cutting capex/guidance — would flip this to a trim.

tactical.bear_case: A 52w low in a downtrending market (below both MAs, death cross) often slices through without a durable bounce; RSI can stay pinned in the 30s for weeks during sustained de-risking. If macro liquidity tightens further or a crypto-specific shock (exchange/stablecoin stress) hits, oversold gets more oversold and the +1.37% bounce fails.
tactical.bull_case: BTC is parked exactly at its 52w low with RSI mid-30s and a green print today — classic short-covering/mean-reversion launchpad. If ETF flows tick positive after the drawdown, a sharp relief rally can materialize given thin liquidity at these levels. Tactical BUYs have been right 100% of the time recently.
tactical.falsifier: A decisive daily close below the $33.41 52w low on rising volume (rather than the bounce holding) would flip this to a momentum-down/trim sign.

positional.bear_case: The 5.9% positional hit rate is the loudest data point: every prior 'deep drawdown buy' kept dropping ~14% over the next 20 days. Price at fresh 52w lows below a death cross is the definition of a trend that hasn't bottomed. If THIS cycle breaks the 4-year pattern (stretching timing, this could be a genuine bear leg), -100 is a wealth-destroying score.
positional.bull_case: BTC -30% below 200DMA with price at the 52w low is statistically where multi-month bottoms form; upside is uncapped if flows reverse. Spot ETF structure means persistent institutional bid can re-emerge fast, and a single positive flow week historically marks the turn. The drawdown is the opportunity.
positional.falsifier: Two consecutive weeks of net positive IBIT/spot-BTC ETF inflows alongside a reclaim of the 50DMA would confirm the bottom and justify pushing back toward -80+; conversely sustained net outflows would flip the sign positive.

strategic.bear_case: If this drawdown signals the 4-year cycle topping later than usual but still topping, the strategic store-of-value momentum thesis weakens and BTC could enter a prolonged bear regime where 'extended_expansion' was actually distribution. Regulatory reversal or a macro real-rate spike (TIPS 2.23%) pressures non-yielding assets.
strategic.bull_case: Structural ETF adoption, potential sovereign/treasury allocation trends, and a friendlier US regulatory posture extend the secular adoption S-curve well beyond the historical 4-year cadence — strategically bullish from a 30%-down base.
strategic.falsifier: A clear regime break — e.g., sustained BTC ETF AUM decline plus regulatory crackdown, or conversely a sovereign-adoption catalyst — would move strategic off neutral.

positional.bear_case: If organic growth stalls at ~1% rather than re-accelerating, the multiple stays compressed and the stock dead-money in the $480-520 band; NIH funding cuts and China life-sciences capex weakness could cap the recovery for several quarters, making the 'discount' a value trap.

strategic.falsifier: Evidence the bioprocessing recovery is real and durable — two consecutive quarters of accelerating organic growth toward 5%+ with rising EPS revisions — would deepen the buy; conversely a forward P/E above 28x with growth decel and NIH/China both deteriorating would flip strategic to trim.

tactical.bear_case: Rates are deflating as Suez reopening restores effective capacity; the daily drop is the start of a freight-rate-driven leg lower and RSI has room to fall further before any bounce.
tactical.bull_case: An oversold single-day -3.4% move on no name-specific news, holding above the 200-day, sets up a quick mean-reversion bounce — my one historical tactical BUY hit +4.8% in 5 days.

tactical.bull_case: BTC ~30% below 200DMA with RSI 33 is exactly where mean-reversion bounces fire; a single positive flow day or macro risk-on (rate-cut repricing) snaps price 8-12% off the lows given thin sell-side liquidity.
tactical.falsifier: A decisive daily close below the $32.84 52w low on accelerating volume and continued ETF outflows would flip tactical positive (trim/avoid).

positional.bear_case: ETF outflows are accelerating, the 4-year cycle may be breaking with a deeper/longer drawdown, and a death-cross with price below both MAs historically precedes further downside; the 11-day failed BUY streak says the dip-buy signal is structurally broken this cycle.
positional.bull_case: BTC down 53% from highs near a major support shelf ($32-33) is where prior cycle bottoms formed; spot ETF structure provides a persistent institutional bid, and any halving-cycle echo or macro liquidity easing produces 2-3x recoveries — positional buyers who caught prior -50% drawdowns were richly rewarded.
positional.falsifier: A weekly close back above the 50DMA (~$40.83) with net-positive sustained ETF inflows would confirm a regime turn and validate stronger buy conviction; conversely sustained sub-$30 with persistent outflows flips this toward neutral/trim.

strategic.bull_case: Structural adoption (sovereign/corporate treasuries, ETF inclusion in model portfolios, potential regulatory clarity) compounds demand against fixed supply; a drawdown deep into a long-term uptrend is a strategic accumulation window with uncapped multi-year upside.
strategic.falsifier: Evidence the secular adoption curve is reversing (sustained multi-quarter ETF AUM decline, hostile regulatory rollback) would flip strategic clearly positive; conversely a new institutional adoption catalyst would push it negative.

tactical.bear_case: VIX at 18.4 with EM names vulnerable to a sudden risk-off could see KOF give back the modest 1.68% pop quickly given thin ADR liquidity.

positional.bear_case: Stock is ~10% above the 200-day after a sustained run; an EM wobble or peso slip could trigger mean-reversion toward SMA50/200 support.

tactical.bear_case: The -24% drawdown is not macro noise — it reflects the reported op-margin pressure (9.6% in the data) and possible program/charge issues; price is below both 50/200 in a death cross with no momentum base, so oversold can stay oversold and a relief bounce fails into supply at SMA50 ($309.8).
tactical.bull_case: LHX is the most beaten-down of the Big-5 primes during a great-power-competition regime with FY budget tailwinds; RSI <40 + 24% drawdown on a backlog compounder is exactly the asymmetric dip that has bounced 75% of the time on the 5d window historically.
tactical.falsifier: RSI pushing back above 50 with a reclaim of SMA50 ($309.8) on volume, or conversely a fresh breakdown to new lows below $245 — either would flip the near-term sign.

positional.bear_case: 9.6% op margin signals real operational trouble (program charges, supply-chain energetics/titanium constraints) rather than a clean valuation pullback; death cross + 32% 52w position means the trend, EPS revisions and institutional flow are all pointed down with no reversal catalyst yet, and a falling knife on a deteriorating margin is the classic positional trap my 27% hit rate warns about.
positional.bull_case: A defense prime down 24% with intact multi-year backlog and DoD expansion tailwinds is a rare entry; if the margin dip is transient (Aerojet integration ramp / timing) the stock re-rates back toward cohort as B/B holds >1.0 and the death cross resolves.
positional.falsifier: Next earnings showing book-to-bill <0.95 with backlog YoY negative and margin guidance cut would confirm thesis break (flip to positive/trim); conversely B/B >1.10 with margin recovery would justify the engine's full -70.

strategic.bull_case: The trailing premium is a margin-trough illusion; on normalized FCF LHX is cheap, and a sustained great-power-competition regime with NDAA expansion and Aerojet propellant scarcity re-rates the smallest prime toward parity — a strategic buy in disguise.
strategic.falsifier: Confirmation that forward P/E sits at a normal -5 to -15% discount to cohort (not premium) alongside margin recovery would flip this to negative; a DoD shift toward sequester/peace-dividend would push it further positive.

tactical.bear_case: The pullback to the 50-day is the start of a QUAL-factor de-risking as the curve steepens (2s10s +31bps) and rates stay restrictive (TIPS 2.19%); a break of the 50-day on volume would invite momentum sellers who chased it to highs.

tactical.falsifier: A decisive close below the 200-day ($468.79) or a sustained break below the 50-day on elevated volume would flip this to trim/negative-trend territory.

positional.bear_case: Very strong USD (DXY 120.4) is a real translation headwind into the next print; if FX drags reported numbers and the +4bps OAS widening continues, the backlog-IRR tailwind reverses and the laggard-vs-AI.PA spread closes the wrong way (LIN underperforms further).
positional.bull_case: LIN trailing AI.PA by ~3pp over the month sets up mean-reversion in the highest-quality name of the oligopoly; tight credit spreads keep project sanctioning IRRs attractive, and a hold of the 50-day with golden-cross structure favors continuation.
positional.falsifier: LIN/peer spreads inverting to LIN leading by >3-4pp (premium re-expansion exhausted) combined with OAS widening through ~120bps would flip the positional sign to trim.

strategic.bull_case: An H2 mega-project win (>$1B blue/green offtake with steel/ammonia/refining), favorable 45V guidance, or a semi-fab on-site contract cluster (TSMC AZ, Samsung TX, Micron NY) re-accelerates backlog — the engine sees contract $ late, and any beat-with-backlog-inflection would re-rate the compounder higher.
strategic.falsifier: Confirmation that ROCE/op-margin compression is structural (multi-quarter downtrend with price/mix ex-FX going negative) would justify staying at/above +24; conversely a confirmed >$1B H2 project or backlog acceleration would flip this negative.

strategic.bull_case: Sovereign/institutional adoption and potential regulatory tailwinds (clearer US crypto framework, possible strategic reserve discussions) extend the cycle far beyond the historical 4-year template; the strategic case is uncapped and a drawdown here is a long-term entry, not a maturity warning.
strategic.falsifier: Evidence the secular adoption curve is reversing — sustained multi-quarter ETF AUM decline plus regulatory rollback — would flip strategic to a clear trim; conversely a major positive regulatory/sovereign catalyst would push strategic negative.

tactical.bear_case: GLNCY keeps diverging from copper — the ADR has fallen while COPX rips, meaning the market is discounting Glencore-specific issues (coal price weakness, cobalt glut, thin ADR volume). tactical.bull_case: Copper surging +3.52% with GLNCY lagging 4.84pp is a classic mean-reversion snapback setup. RSI 34 oversold plus a strong lead indicator (LME/COPX) argues for a bounce back toward the 50-day as the ADR catches up to the underlying commodity move.
tactical.falsifier: If GLNCY breaks below the 200-day ($12.49) on rising volume while copper holds its gains, the catch-up thesis is dead and the tactical sign flips positive (trim).

positional.bear_case: The COPX/GLNCY spread has been persistently negative and NOT reverting — the ADR keeps underperforming copper, meaning coal (25% of book) and the ESG discount, plus weak zinc/nickel/cobalt, are offsetting copper strength. Positional layer is graded POOR; the catch-up signal is likely noise here.
positional.bull_case: GLNCY at 1.62x book with a diversified copper/coal/trading mix into a copper up-cycle and tight credit spreads is well-positioned. If Chinese stimulus/property stabilizes, copper leadership pulls the whole complex up and GLNCY closes the 4.84pp gap sharply given its operating leverage.
positional.falsifier: COPX/GLNCY spread turning positive (GLNCY leading copper) for several sessions, or HY OAS widening past ~3.5bps signaling demand deterioration, would flip the positional sign.

strategic.bear_case: The trough P/E may be masking a structurally lower-earning franchise: coal faces terminal decline/ESG divestment pressure, cobalt and nickel are in oversupply, and if copper's rally is speculative (not demand-driven) the whole thesis is a value trap. At 1.62x book there's no asset-value cushion if commodities roll over.
strategic.bull_case: Copper structural deficit + energy-transition demand with constrained supply gives GLNCY multi-year earnings upside from trough. The trading arm ($2-4B EBITDA on minimal capital, worth 8-12x) is priced near zero — sum-of-parts is materially above the current price if any of these rerate.
strategic.falsifier: Sustained Chinese property/PMI contraction dragging LME copper back below ~$4/lb, or a coal-price collapse, would flip the strategic sign to trim as the trough deepens structurally rather than recovers.

tactical.bear_case: The +3.35% pop is a dead-cat bounce inside a confirmed death-cross downtrend; RSI 42 has plenty of room to roll back toward 30, and a failed bounce off the 52w low often precedes a fresh leg down.
tactical.bull_case: Short-window mean reversion has worked 100% of the time for IBIT tactically; RSI lifting off the low with a strong daily gain often marks a local momentum turn, and oversold BTC snaps back violently.
tactical.falsifier: BTC closes back below the recent $32.84 52w low with RSI breaking under 35 — would confirm downside continuation and flip tactical to neutral/sell.

positional.bear_case: Death cross intact, 51% off highs, restrictive real rates, and a 31-sample track record showing this exact signal loses ~14% over 20 days — the drawdown is a trend, not a dip, and could extend well below the 52w low before flows return.
positional.bull_case: BTC is a momentum asset with uncapped upside; buying 27% below the 200DMA near 52w lows historically front-runs violent cycle recoveries, and if ETF flows flip positive the reflexive squeeze is large.
positional.falsifier: Sustained positive ETF net inflows for a week AND BTC reclaiming the 50DMA ($40.28) would validate a bottom and justify pushing back toward -80; conversely a decisive break of $32.84 on accelerating outflows flips me toward neutral/sell.

strategic.bear_case: This cycle may be breaking the 4-year pattern to the downside — if ETF adoption has already pulled forward demand, the structural bid weakens and the deep drawdown becomes a secular top rather than a mid-cycle pause.
strategic.bull_case: Structural adoption (spot ETFs, sovereign/corporate treasuries, potential reserve status) is still early-innings and could drive a multi-year re-rating that dwarfs cycle timing; being months-deep in the cycle is irrelevant if adoption keeps compounding.
strategic.falsifier: A sustained shift in the regime — either Fed easing reviving risk appetite (bullish, push negative) or a durable regulatory/adoption reversal (bearish, push positive) — would give strategic a real sign.

positional.bull_case: Sustained nearshoring-driven Mexican wage growth and formal employment support staples volumes; a strong peso plus KOF's pricing power keeps the uptrend and dividend growth compounding, arguing for staying long weight.
positional.falsifier: A decisive break below the SMA50 ($105.63) on rising volume, coincident with MXN weakening past ~18, would flip the positional sign to net-add-caution/positive.

tactical.bear_case: The 2.21% single-day move is ~2-3x LIN's normal daily range, RSI pushing 66 into a fresh all-time high — a garden-variety pullback of 1-2% to fill the gap is the path of least resistance from here.
tactical.bull_case: Golden-cross intact, price above both MAs, quality/QUAL factor bid persists in a NEUTRAL-VIX tape; compounders at highs tend to stay at highs, and breakout above prior 52w high can attract momentum flow.
tactical.falsifier: A close back below the 50-day SMA ($509.76) on elevated volume would flip tactical from mildly positive to buy-the-dip negative.

positional.bear_case: At 100.7% of range with RSI 66, the positional setup is priced for perfection — any earnings wobble or FX drag (DXY very strong) leaves no valuation cushion, making trim the asymmetric choice for a name with zero margin of safety.

positional.falsifier: LIN/APD spread widening beyond ~+3pp (LIN outperforming to a stretched premium) OR BBB OAS blowing out past ~130bps would flip this to a trim signal.

tactical.bear_case: Price has broken below SMA200 ($15.71) too, meaning both moving averages now overhead — 'oversold' in a confirmed downtrend often means 'about to get more oversold.' tactical.bull_case: RSI 31.7 with a +1.9% up day suggests a short-term bottom forming; WTI firm at $72 and 9.4% yield provide a floor for value buyers. Any relief on political risk or an OPEC+ supply signal snaps oversold names back fast.
tactical.falsifier: A close back above SMA50 ($19.04) on rising volume, or conversely a decisive break below $14 on a political headline, would flip the tactical read.

positional.bear_case: Being below both MAs after breaking the 200-day is a bearish trend confirmation, not a value setup — momentum funds sell these. Brazilian fiscal deterioration and BRL at 5.16 add currency drag on USD-denominated ADR returns even if oil holds.
positional.bull_case: 22% below SMA50 with oil stable is a rare dislocation; historically PBR snaps back violently once the political overhang clears. High dividend yield attracts income buyers at these levels, providing support.

strategic.falsifier: A credible, durable dividend-policy commitment or oil breaking sustainably above $85 (deepening rather than peaking earnings) would flip strategic to buy; conversely an explicit government dividend/capex raid confirms the trim.

tactical.bear_case: TMO has rallied hard off lows and RSI 65.8 is approaching the tightened 70 ceiling; a single hot inflation print or a weak DHR/Sartorius pre-announce could trigger fast mean reversion in a name up ~29% from trough. XBI's +2.25% pop can reverse just as quickly.
tactical.bull_case: XBI +2.25% and TMO outperforming DHR shows biotech sympathy turning constructive; a -19% drawdown compounder catching a quality bid with VIX benign (16.59) and credit tight (HY 2.74) can grind higher into any positive peer read.
tactical.falsifier: RSI pushing >70 with TMO stalling below the $526 SMA200 on a down-day for XBI would flip this toward trim.

positional.bear_case: The stock is up ~29% off the low and pressed right against SMA200 resistance ($526.45) — a common rejection zone. If the Q1 2026 print confirms only ~1% organic growth without visible re-acceleration signals, the positional 'on sale' narrative loses its catalyst and the name could stall/retrace toward SMA50.
positional.bull_case: A clean break above SMA200 on cycle-bottoming confirmation flips the technical structure bullish; historical positional BUYs on TMO have hit 100% of the time over 20d with +6.6% avg — a real edge on a quality compounder emerging from a multi-quarter destock trough.
positional.falsifier: A decisive break back below SMA50 (~$473) on deteriorating bioprocessing/peer commentary would flip the sign to trim.

Discount to DHR closes; Wave 4 AI biologics bioproduction adds a new secular leg.
strategic.falsifier: Confirmed China capex collapse plus NIH budget cuts plus a bioprocessing re-destock in the next earnings print would flip this to neutral/trim.

tactical.bear_case: The +4.53% pop is a rate-headline spike; freight rates are notoriously mean-reverting, and if Red Sea transits normalize (ceasefire/Houthi de-escalation), effective capacity floods back and spot rates collapse within weeks, unwinding the move.

positional.bear_case: Massive orderbook delivery — the container fleet is growing ~7-8% annually through 2026 with record newbuild deliveries; once Red Sea normalizes, structural overcapacity crushes rates and the golden cross rolls over hard.

tactical.falsifier: An RSI move above ~70 (overbought, tactical trim) or a sharp gap-down below the 50-day (oversold buy) would move the tactical score off zero.

tactical.falsifier: A close back below the recent swing low (~$249 area) on expanding volume, erasing today's rally, would flip this to a positive (trim/avoid) tactical read.

positional.bear_case: The death cross + 9.61% op margin is not a 'compounder on sale' — it may be the market correctly pricing structural margin compression, program cost overruns, or supply-chain (energetics/titanium) drag that persists for multiple quarters. positional.bull_case: LHX at 40.7% of its 52w range with a $300+ price and intact multi-year backlog is a rare entry for the cheapest DoD prime; if B/B is >1.0 and DoD budget moves to real expansion (NDAA/supplementals), the stock re-rates toward cohort and the drawdown fully reverses.

positional.bear_case: Being pinned at 99.7% of 52w high with no peer premium compression means positional risk/reward is asymmetric to the downside — a mean-reversion or QUAL rotation unwind hits the extended name hardest.
positional.bull_case: Tight credit spreads and intact golden-cross uptrend with peers inline mean the compounder trend is durable; buyers step in on any dip and the 52w-high breakout confirms accumulation.
positional.falsifier: LIN/APD spread widening materially (>+3pp premium expansion) or a break below the 50-day MA — either would flip the positional read from mild-buy toward neutral/trim.

strategic.bull_case: A mega-scale H2 project win (>$1B offtake with steel/ammonia take-or-pay) or a semi capex super-cycle confirmation would re-accelerate backlog; LIN's oligopoly pricing power and inflation pass-through make the premium multiple sustainable and the FX headwind transitory.

tactical.bull_case: RSI 27 with WTI holding $72 and price sitting right on the 200-day ($15.71) is a textbook reversion zone; any oil uptick or reassuring dividend headline snaps a fast 5-8% squeeze given the 9.4% carry that attracts dip buyers.
tactical.falsifier: Two consecutive up days reclaiming the SMA50 zone toward $16+ on rising volume would flip this to a momentum-follow SELL-side lean; conversely a break below the 200-day with a political catalyst would deepen the buy.

positional.bear_case: below_both is a valid trend-follow SELL structure; PBR.A can drift for months when Lula interference fears dominate, and the dividend-yield floor is illusory if Petrobras is forced into higher social/refining capex that cuts the payout.
positional.bull_case: A 9.4% yield with pre-salt breakeven ~$35/bbl and WTI at $72 means huge free cash generation; at $14.53 the market is pricing peak political pessimism, and any de-escalation reprices the stock materially higher.

strategic.bull_case: If the 2026 Brazilian political cycle shifts market-friendly or oil stays firm, a 4.8x P/E with a 9.4% yield is deep value; state oils globally re-rate when payout credibility is restored, offering multi-year total return well above the index.

tactical.bull_case: XBI +2.52% today with 90d biotech thaw underway; if TMO breaks the 200-day at $526 on volume it confirms trend reversal and the -18.7% drawdown resolves upward toward $560+. Sympathy flows just turning on.

tactical.bear_case: The +4.53% spike exhausts near-term buying, RSI rolls over from mid-range, and the stock mean-reverts back toward the 50-day as freight-rate optimism fades — a classic shipping head-fake into a rate downcycle.
tactical.bull_case: The rally is the start of a breakout on improving spot freight rates (Red Sea reroutes still tightening capacity) and the golden cross above both MAs draws momentum/trend followers, extending the move.
tactical.falsifier: A close back below the 50-day SMA ($12.14) on rising volume would flip this from mild-trim to a buy-the-dip tactical read.

positional.bear_case: Suez/Red Sea normalizes as ceasefire holds, vessels return to shorter routes, effective capacity surges into an already-oversupplied orderbook, and spot rates collapse — GSCPI falls below 0.5 and the positional tailwind evaporates within weeks.
positional.bull_case: Red Sea disruption persists or worsens, GSCPI stays elevated, spot rates re-accelerate, and Maersk's above-both-MA trend compounds as consensus upgrades 2026 EBITDA on sustained higher rates.
positional.falsifier: GSCPI dropping below 0.5 alongside a double-digit weekly WCI decline would flip this to a clear trim (positive) score.

strategic.falsifier: P/B rising above ~1.0 (re-rating to/above book) OR a confirmed structural rate collapse driving Ocean into sustained operating losses would flip this to neutral/trim.

tactical.bear_case: The +5.28% pop is a one-day gap on sentiment; gaps into strength often mean-revert 1-3% over the following sessions, and RSI has room to fall from 54. Chasing here is poor entry.

tactical.falsifier: A second consecutive up-day of >3% pushing RSI above 70 would flip this toward a clearer trim; conversely a same-magnitude gap-fill down would flip it to a buy.

positional.bull_case: The golden-cross uptrend is intact and accelerating; ASML compounders that hold well above rising MAs rarely give back gains without a fundamental shock, and the trend structure supports adding on any dip rather than trimming.
positional.falsifier: A break below the SMA50 ($1655) on WFE/capex-cut news would flip this positive; a clean consolidation above $1850 that resets the extension would flip it modestly negative.

tactical.falsifier: A sharp 10Y move (>25bps in a day) or an RSI break below 30 that creates a genuine oversold, fade-able dislocation would flip this off neutral.

positional.bear_case: 58bps spread means ENB is priced richly relative to risk-free — with TIPS at 2.25% and 2s10s only +31bps, an income buyer has better risk-adjusted alternatives; further yield compression from here has poor forward returns (my 0% hit rate confirms buying near these levels underperforms).

positional.bear_case: MA below both death cross, 90d EPS revisions likely negative given margin compression, and 20% historical hit rate on my own positional buys — the trend is my enemy and there is no confirmed inflection in book-to-bill or margin to catch. Catching a falling knife on a name where the deterioration is operational, not sentiment, is the losing trade the calibration data is screaming about.
positional.bull_case: LHX is the smallest prime at a historically justified discount, backlog compounders re-rate hard when margins turn; if book-to-bill prints >1.10 and LHARRIS Next cost-out program delivers, the -22% drawdown becomes the entry point of the year with DoD budget expanding and Golden Dome/munitions demand accelerating.
