positional.falsifier: Yield spread widening back above ~150bps (via a 10Y drop toward 3.5% or ENB selling off to a ~6% yield) would flip this to a positional buy.

strategic.bear_case: 24.7x P/E and a 5.0% yield near the compressed end of its range mean the market has already paid up for the LNG/dividend story; with real yields at 2.31%, a hold-forever income name offers little total-return edge and meaningful opportunity cost vs cheaper book positions.
strategic.bull_case: LNG Canada Phase 1 export ramp plus potential Phase 2 FID, Gulf Coast gas expansion, and the acquired US gas utilities give ENB a rare visible multi-year EBITDA growth runway; if the BoC/Fed ease into 2026, the entire spread re-rates and ENB compounds dividend + capital appreciation.
strategic.falsifier: A dividend-growth guidance cut, a stalled/cancelled major LNG or pipeline project, or a move to a ~6%+ yield (top of 5yr range) would flip strategic to a buy.

tactical.bear_case: Price is still below the 50-day and today's 2.68% pop could be a dead-cat bounce in a downtrending name; failure to reclaim $15 keeps the intermediate trend down and RSI could roll back toward 30s if copper stalls on soft Chinese data.
tactical.bull_case: RSI 42 with a strong up day and outperformance vs COPX suggests accumulation; a reclaim of the 50-day would trigger momentum buyers and the name is compressing toward a breakout above the $15 pivot.
tactical.falsifier: A close back below the 200-day SMA ($12.63) on rising volume would flip this to a trim/sell tactical stance; conversely a decisive reclaim of $15.02 would deepen the buy.

positional.bear_case: Coal (25% of EBITDA) faces structural ESG derating and softening thermal/met prices; Chinese property remains weak and PMI borderline, which could cap copper and drag the whole complex. Below the 50-day means the positional trend is not yet confirmed up.
positional.bull_case: Copper tightness plus trading-arm optionality (volatility is good for the marketing business) plus tight credit spreads point to a demand-healthy regime; GLNCY leading COPX means the market is re-rating the diversified model, a positive medium-term inflection.
positional.falsifier: LME copper breaking below ~$8,500/t or HY OAS widening sharply (regime turn) would flip positional to positive/trim; sustained reclaim of 50-day with copper >$10,500 deepens the buy.

strategic.bear_case: Coal exposure creates persistent ESG discount and capital-allocation uncertainty (spin vs retain); a hard-landing in China or global recession would push copper into surplus and gut the mining EBITDA while the trading arm alone can't carry the valuation. P/B 1.64 means you're not buying below replacement cost.
strategic.bull_case: Structural copper deficit into 2027-2030 from underinvestment plus energy-transition demand makes GLNCY's copper book increasingly scarce and valuable; trading arm consistently delivers $3-4B EBITDA with optionality on volatility, and management has a strong capital-return track record via buybacks. Trough earnings today = maximum upside as the cycle recovers.
strategic.falsifier: A structural copper surplus emerging (rising LME inventories + Chinese demand collapse) or a debt-funded value-destructive acquisition would flip strategic to trim; confirmation of copper deficit + coal demerger unlocking value deepens the buy.

tactical.bear_case: Price is below both MAs in a death cross; neutral RSI within a downtrend often resolves lower as sellers retain control, so momentum favors continuation down — argues for a positive tactical tilt.
tactical.bull_case: A +1.37% up day off the 52w low zone with RSI recovering off oversold could be the start of a short-term mean-reversion bounce back toward the 50DMA — argues negative.
tactical.falsifier: RSI dropping <30 on a capitulation flush (flip decisively negative/buy) OR a reclaim + hold above the 50DMA with rising volume (tactical momentum turn).

positional.bear_case: Death cross intact, 23% below 200DMA in a confirmed downtrend, decelerating flows, and a 5.9% historical hit rate on these exact buys — downtrends overshoot, so the score belongs closer to zero.
positional.bull_case: 8.9% of 52w range is genuinely washed out; BTC's biggest rallies come from deep sub-200DMA drawdowns, and the ETF structural bid can reverse flows violently — supports the quant -100.
positional.falsifier: Accelerating ETF net outflows + new 52w low below $32.84 flips positive; confirmed positive net inflows validates deeper buy.

strategic.bear_case: If 4-year cycle holds, month 27 extended_expansion precedes a cyclical top — current drawdown could be the first leg of a bear, justifying trim.
strategic.bull_case: ETF adoption changes demand elasticity; regulatory clarity and institutional accumulation could extend the cycle beyond the template, making these levels long-term accumulation.
strategic.falsifier: Sustained LTH distribution + persistent multi-month ETF outflows + break below prior-cycle structural support flips strategic positive.

tactical.bear_case: Stock is making fresh 52w lows and below both MAs (death cross) — momentum is genuinely broken; a weak July 16 procedure print or early transition quantification could gap it another leg down, and pre-print buyers get run over.
tactical.bull_case: A -32.6% drawdown with RSI 43 (not even oversold) into a July 16 print where consensus ~$2.48 EPS is beatable and 2026 dV guide of 13.5-15.5% procedure growth is likely reaffirmed — any guide reaffirm triggers a violent relief bounce off the low.
tactical.falsifier: July 16 procedure growth prints <12% WITH competitor US procedure-share evidence, or transition quantified as material — that would flip this to a trim/sell tactical.

positional.bear_case: Below both 50 and 200 SMA with a death cross and fresh 52w lows is a textbook broken chart; buying max-size into confirmed downward momentum ahead of a print has burned the model (yesterday's buy was wrong).
positional.bull_case: Franchise trading at 5% of its 52w range with an active devices-sector bid (IHI +4pp) and forward valuation compressed — mean reversion in a moat compounder from these levels historically snaps back hard once selling exhausts.
positional.falsifier: A confirmed reclaim of the 50-day ($423.91) on positive EPS revisions post-print would deepen the buy; conversely negative 30/90d EPS revisions plus procedure deceleration would flip positional toward neutral/trim.

strategic.bear_case: The 2027 transition could be quantified as material on July 16 (>mid-single-digit revenue headwind), and if Hugo/Ottava start displacing US accounts the entire multiple thesis re-rates lower — a 48x PE still has enormous downside if growth durability breaks.
strategic.bull_case: Forward PE ~40x for a two-decade moat with 86% recurring revenue, 11,400 installed base, and 40M-procedure data lead is a rare own-history discount; transition quantified as manageable would be a relief re-rating catalyst worth 15-20%.
strategic.falsifier: Management quantifying the 2027 transition as material (>mid-single-digit % revenue) OR measurable US procedure-share loss to Hugo — either flips strategic to positive/trim.

tactical.bear_case: Price below the 50-day and rolling over from the $116 high; if MXN weakens on a dovish Banxico or US-Mexico tariff headline, KOF ADR could slip toward the 200-day quickly, and a sub-50 RSI has room to fall further.
tactical.bull_case: RSI 43 near the lower half of its range plus a bounce off support with today's +0.84% could mark a short-term reversal; a firm peso print would lift the ADR mechanically off this level.
tactical.falsifier: A sharp MXN move (>2-3%) in either direction on a Banxico decision or tariff headline would give the tactical layer a real directional edge and flip it off zero.

positional.bear_case: KOF has been grinding lower off the $116 high; a still-restrictive Banxico and soft Mexican consumer discretionary spend could keep the ADR range-bound to lower over the coming weeks, and PET/sugar input cost creep pressures bottler margins.
positional.bull_case: Defensive staples with 3.76% yield and cheap 12.8x multiple offers downside cushion; any peso strength or a reassuring KO parent volume readout could pull the ADR back toward the 50-day at $106.
positional.falsifier: A break below the 200-day ($98.51) accompanied by MXN weakening past ~18.0 would flip this to a clear trim/positive setup.

strategic.bear_case: MXN faces real risk from US tariff rhetoric and a Banxico easing cycle that narrows the carry; a weaker peso directly deflates USD ADR earnings, and Mexican consumer staples volumes are mature/low-growth, capping the compounder story.
strategic.bull_case: Structural nearshoring, resilient staples demand, best-in-class bottler scale vs Arca Continental, disciplined capital return, and a depressed 12.8x multiple leave meaningful re-rating and total-return upside if MXN holds or firms.
strategic.falsifier: A sustained MXN break weaker than ~18.5 (tariff shock or aggressive Banxico cuts) or a margin miss from input-cost inflation would flip the strategic case to positive.

tactical.bear_case: The drawdown is fundamentally justified, not technical noise — LHX is down 6.6% over 30d while the entire defense cohort is UP, meaning this is company-specific (margin miss / integration drag) and knives can keep falling toward the $256 low. Death cross with no RSI oversold reading means no mean-reversion trigger is armed.
tactical.bull_case: A -23.8% drawdown in the smallest prime during an active defense bid (ITA +2.7pp vs SPY) with the cohort rallying sets up sharp mean-reversion; any single program award or B/B reacceleration snaps the 9.5pp cohort gap shut fast.
tactical.falsifier: A decisive close back above the 50-day ($303.62) on rising volume, or RSI reclaiming 55+, would confirm the downtrend has broken and flip this toward a momentum-follow trim rather than a dip-buy.

positional.bear_case: The 52w-low positioning is a value trap: margins under pressure (9.61%), cohort rotating AWAY from LHX specifically, death cross intact. My 17.6% historical hit rate on positional buys here proves 'deep drawdown' has been a persistent losing bet — the market is repricing LHX's earnings power, not overreacting.
positional.bull_case: LHX at 26.9% of its 52w range while defense is bid (ITA +2.7pp vs SPY) and backlog compounders re-rate off budget expansion — a B/B >1.05 print or Golden Dome / SRM award reprices the smallest, cheapest-on-forward prime sharply higher; cohort discount widening is the re-rating fuel.
positional.falsifier: A book-to-bill print below 0.95 with backlog YoY turning negative, or a DoD budget shift to CR/sequester, would confirm structural backlog erosion and flip this to a trim.

strategic.bear_case: LHX trading at a +23% premium to a cohort where it should sit at a discount, on margins compressed to 9.61%, is a mean-reversion setup — as EPS recovers the multiple looks even richer, and forward estimates could disappoint if Aerojet integration and supply-chain (energetics/titanium) constraints persist.
strategic.bull_case: The 'premium' is a trailing-earnings mirage — normalize margins to the 14-16% prime range and forward P/E drops into the discount zone; sustained great-power competition + NDAA expansion + SRM duopoly make LHX a structural multi-year backlog compounder worth owning through the margin trough.
strategic.falsifier: Margins recovering back toward 14%+ with rising EPS revisions AND the forward P/E slipping to a genuine discount vs cohort would flip strategic to a BUY; conversely a peace-dividend regime shift would deepen the trim.

tactical.bear_case: RSI 56 with price at 89% of 52w range and a fresh +2% two-day pop leaves LIN slightly extended near $548 highs; a mega-cap growth-narrative rotation (AI names) could pull QUAL flow away and stall LIN short-term.
tactical.bull_case: Quality bid is accelerating (QUAL +1.4pp/30d) into a low-VIX melt-up; LIN's steady trend above rising 50/200 MAs is exactly the setup that grinds higher with no volatility for weeks.
tactical.falsifier: A break below the 50-day SMA ($512) on rising volume, or QUAL factor flow flipping negative vs SPY, would flip this to a trim-side (positive) tactical read.

positional.bear_case: The +1bp uptick in BBB OAS, if it becomes a trend, would be the first crack in the capex-IRR tailwind; combined with the near-highs positioning, mean-reversion risk grows if the peer premium stops compressing and re-widens.
positional.bull_case: LIN leading AI.PA by 1.34pp with an inline APD spread and tight credit signals the market is rewarding LIN's superior contracted-revenue mix; continued spread leadership plus a healthy golden-cross structure supports further relative outperformance within the portfolio.
positional.falsifier: A close below the 200-day SMA ($471) or BBB OAS widening sharply (>120bps) breaking the credit tailwind would flip this positive.

strategic.bear_case: If op margin compression at 26.48% is NOT purely FX-driven — e.g., green-H2 projects diluting returns or specialty-gas pricing softening — then the deserved premium at 34x P/E is vulnerable to a de-rate toward APD/AI.PA's ~31x, roughly a 10% valuation air-pocket, especially with DXY a persistent earnings headwind.
strategic.bull_case: A >$1B hydrogen mega-project win or a cluster of semi-fab on-site contracts (TSMC AZ, Samsung TX, Micron NY) would re-accelerate backlog and validate the premium; with real yields only mildly restrictive (TIPS 2.31%) and quality bid active, LIN can compound the premium higher rather than de-rate.
strategic.falsifier: Confirmation that ROCE/margin compression is structural (backlog turning negative AND like-for-like price/mix ex-FX going negative in the next print) would flip this to a stronger sell; conversely a mega-project H2 announcement plus backlog acceleration would flip it to a buy.

tactical.bear_case: Re-rating is already underway (duopoly +5.4pp vs SPY over 30d); the fear-driven mean-reversion trade is largely spent, so buying here chases a move rather than fading fear. RSI 57.75 has room to roll over and the stock is still below its 200-day (499 vs 529), meaning the intermediate trend is unconfirmed — a stall near the 200-day is a natural fade point.
tactical.bull_case: A -13% drawdown on a ~0.83-beta tollbooth with cross-border volumes intact is exactly the archetype setup; VIX at 15.84 and HY OAS ultra-tight (2.7bps) signal a benign risk backdrop where quality compounders resume trending. Reclaiming the 200-day at $529 would confirm the uptrend and draw QUAL flow.
tactical.falsifier: A close back above the 200-day SMA ($529.58) with RSI pushing toward 65 would flip this to a mild-trim (positive) as the drawdown edge disappears; conversely a break below the 50-day ($499) on volume would deepen the buy setup.

positional.bear_case: Above_50_below_200 means the intermediate trend is still broken; the stock has been unable to reclaim the 200-day and 42.7% 52w position can persist for months if consumer spend and cross-border decelerate into a slowing macro. If cross-border prints <10% next quarter the 'compounder on sale' thesis weakens materially.
positional.bull_case: Twin at parity with V is unusual — MA is the faster grower and normally commands a 10-20% premium; parity plus a 13% drawdown with no fundamental break is a classic re-rate-to-fair-value setup. Positive EPS revisions and VAS >20% growth mean forward multiple compression is doing the work while earnings compound.
positional.falsifier: A quarterly print showing cross-border volume growth decelerating below ~8% (spend cycle rolling) or a clean break/hold below the 50-day would flip positional toward neutral-to-trim; conversely reclaiming the 200-day confirms and deepens conviction.

strategic.bear_case: Higher-for-longer rates (10Y 4.56%, real 2.31%) structurally compress premium multiples for long-duration compounders, so parity vs V may be a new regime rather than a dislocation. If stablecoin adoption at real merchants (not pilots) becomes measurable or interchange legislation advances to a floor vote, the multiple re-rates DOWN and the tollbooth narrative genuinely cracks.
strategic.bull_case: Forward PE ~26-27x with PEG ~1.5, twin premium at parity, cross-border double-digit, VAS ~20%+ — every strategic lever points to a re-rate toward the historical 10-20% V premium and 30x+ multiple. Peak-narrative/no-evidence disruption cycles have historically resolved with MA re-rating higher as volumes never notice; MA leading the stablecoin buildout converts the threat into a revenue line.
strategic.falsifier: Evidence flips from 'anecdotal' to MATERIAL real-economy merchant stablecoin displacement, OR interchange/network-fee regulation moves to 'advancing/passed' at a floor vote — either would flip strategic to positive (trim). A forward PE re-rating above ~34x with decelerating volumes would also flip the sign.

tactical.bear_case: The 30% drawdown reflects a genuine AI-capex ROI reset — the market is repricing hyperscaler spend that isn't converting to Azure acceleration. RSI at 46 (not oversold) means there is room to keep bleeding toward the $349 low; catching a falling knife in a de-rating secular story is exactly how tactical buys lose.
tactical.bull_case: A quality mega-cap down 30% with active cohort rotation pressure and a quality bid is a classic mean-reversion coil; any Azure reassurance or peer capex discipline headline snaps it back 5-8% fast, and yesterday's +1.26% suggests the bounce may already be starting.
tactical.falsifier: RSI breaking back below 40 with price undercutting the $349 52w low on heavy volume would flip this to trim/neutral — it would confirm the drawdown is trending, not reverting.

positional.bear_case: Death-cross with price 13% below the 200-day and 5% below the 50-day means the trend is down and bases take weeks; positional BUY signals on MSFT have hit only 20% of the time recently, so the -81 quant is likely overconfident on a knife that keeps falling into earnings.
positional.bull_case: MSFT at 17% of range with 46.75% margins intact is a rare franchise-quality dislocation; historically these compounder drawdowns fully retrace within 20 days once the death-cross exhausts, and the deep cohort P/E discount gives fundamental buyers a floor.
positional.falsifier: Azure cloud growth printing below 22% at the next quarter, or capex guidance rising while peers cut — that would convert the drawdown from a dislocation into a justified de-rating and flip the sign to trim.

strategic.bear_case: Forward P/E near 28-30x on decelerating Azure growth is not cheap — the trailing 22.5x discount is a mirage from a strong prior year. With TIPS at 2.31% (restrictive) pressuring long-duration multiples and OpenAI partnership economics (funding commitments, AGI clause, revenue share) an open-ended liability, the strategic setup is fairly valued at best, not an exceptional buy.
strategic.bull_case: After a 30% drawdown, MSFT's forward multiple has compressed toward the low end of its 3Y range while Azure remains the primary enterprise AI-infra beneficiary with OpenAI as proprietary distribution and 46.75% margins — a rare chance to own the best AI-infra compounder at a below-average forward valuation.
strategic.falsifier: OpenAI partnership restructuring that dilutes MSFT's IP access/exclusivity, or forward P/E re-expanding above 35x on a relief rally without Azure re-acceleration — either would remove the strategic value edge and flip toward neutral/trim.

tactical.bear_case: A -48.8% drawdown that reclaims only the 50-day while sitting far below the 200-day is a classic bear-market rally in a broken uptrend; RSI 54 has room to roll over and revisit the $81 low if the next print disappoints. Rate pressure at 1.32% real keeps a bid off long-duration SaaS.
tactical.bull_case: Price is +4% over 30d vs a flat cohort and has reclaimed the 50-day SMA off a rare 20th-percentile 52w position; capitulation lows in $80B+ quality franchises historically snap back hard when any positive catalyst hits, and VIX at 15.8 signals no macro stress forcing further liquidation.
tactical.falsifier: A close back below the 50-day SMA (~$102) on rising volume, or RSI breaking below 40, would flip this to trim/neutral as the stabilization thesis fails.

positional.bear_case: The 48% de-rate is the market correctly repricing NOW from a 65x forward premium SaaS multiple toward a lower-growth reality — if cRPO has decelerated toward the high teens, there is no floor at $107 and the fair value is materially lower. My 0% positional hit rate says the down-trend has persistently continued.
positional.bull_case: NOW's ~80% gross margin, ~30% non-GAAP op margin and 20%+ subscription growth franchise trading at a 20th-percentile 52w position with EPS revisions likely still positive is a rare entry into a best-in-class compounder; federal vertical wins and Now Assist Pro Plus monetization provide asymmetric upside catalysts the price does not reflect.
positional.falsifier: A cRPO print below 18% YoY (thesis air-pocket) or a downward EPS revision trend would flip this to neutral/trim; conversely confirmation of cRPO re-acceleration would justify the full engine conviction.

strategic.bear_case: If real rates stay above 1.3% and drift toward 2%, and CRM/MSFT successfully commoditize agentic workflow automation, NOW's premium multiple continues to compress structurally — a 48% drawdown can extend when a secular moat premium is being questioned, not just a cyclical dip.
strategic.bull_case: Post de-rate, forward PE is likely back in the fair 55-65x zone with EV/Sales normalized; if the agentic-AI cycle is genuinely early_commercialization (Now Assist Pro Plus attach rising, AI Agent Studio scaling, federal wins), the strategic secular growth story remains intact and the current price is a durable entry into a quality compounder.
strategic.falsifier: Evidence that agentic-AI commercialization has entered saturation (Now Assist ASP/attach stalling) or a major federal contract loss would push this decisively to trim; confirmation of early-phase monetization inflection would flip it to buy.

tactical.bear_case: WTI is soft in the high-$60s with OPEC+ unwinding cuts and adding barrels into a soft-demand macro; a break below $65 would drag PBR.A tactically lower given its high oil beta, and price sits below both MAs signaling the tape is not yet friendly.
tactical.bull_case: Price bounced 1.3% off a deeply oversold structure (-16.5% below SMA50), and any Lula-friendly dividend confirmation or a WTI recovery toward $73 could spark a fast mean-reversion rally in a name that moves 5%+ on single headlines.
tactical.falsifier: A sharp WTI move (>5% in either direction) or a Petrobras-specific headline (dividend/CEO/fuel-pricing) that decisively breaks the current range and pushes RSI out of the 40-55 band.

positional.bear_case: Below-both-MAs is a genuine downtrend; if WTI keeps sliding and Lula pressures for higher domestic refining capex or caps fuel pricing, the SMA200 support at $15.82 fails and there is little technical support until the low teens.
positional.bull_case: At the SMA200 with a 9.4% yield and 4.9x earnings, PBR.A is priced for bad news; a stabilization in oil plus continued extraordinary dividends could drive mean reversion back toward SMA50 near $18.60 (~20% upside), a classic EM-oil accumulation setup.
positional.falsifier: A decisive close below SMA200 ($15.82) on rising volume with WTI breaking $65, OR a sustained reclaim of SMA50 that flips the MA structure bullish.

strategic.bear_case: Peak-earnings 5x P/E plus a Lula administration that has repeatedly signaled it wants Petrobras to invest rather than distribute — a formal shift in dividend policy or a fuel-pricing intervention below import parity would structurally impair the entire em_state_oil_dividend thesis and justify a much higher trim score.
strategic.bull_case: Pre-salt breakeven near $35 means the ordinary dividend is safe at $69.6 WTI; if Petrobras sustains extraordinary payouts and oil holds, total return of ~10%+ yield plus buybacks beats most of the book, and political risk is already heavily discounted in the 4.9x multiple versus IOC peers at 8-12x.
strategic.falsifier: A concrete government action redirecting cash flow away from shareholders (dividend policy change, below-parity fuel pricing mandate, or a large value-destructive capex/M&A commitment) would push the score sharply more positive; conversely a formal reaffirmation of the current shareholder-return framework alongside oil stability would flip it negative.

tactical.bear_case: RSI near 65 after a 15% monthly move means TMO is short-term overbought; a single soft peer print (Sartorius/Repligen) or an XBI pullback (already -4% today) could trigger a 3-5% mean-reversion, so chasing here has poor entry.
tactical.bull_case: Still -18% off the 52w high with QUAL bid active and biotech funding thawing — TMO remains in the compounder buy zone and momentum plus quality flows could carry it back toward SMA200 ($527.5) quickly.
tactical.falsifier: A sustained break back below SMA50 ($477.53) on heavy volume, or XBI 30d rolling negative, would flip this to a trim/avoid short-term stance.

positional.bear_case: Positional layer reliability is grading POOR (28.9%) portfolio-wide, and TMO has now rallied 15% in a month — the easy positional gain from the drawdown may already be in the price, with SMA200 acting as overhead resistance before cycle confirmation arrives.
positional.bull_case: XBI 90d +24.4% is a genuine leading tailwind; if bioprocessing re-accelerates off the ~1% organic trough toward 5%+ over the next two quarters, TMO reclaims SMA200 and the multiple re-rates, delivering the historical +7-8% swing move.
positional.falsifier: XBI 90d rolling back below +10% (regime shift from thawing to neutral/frozen) plus a peer (DHR/Sartorius) guiding bioprocessing weaker would flip the positional sign toward trim.

strategic.bull_case: TMO is the ASML of life sciences with a widening moat, PPD/CRO cross-sell, and emerging Wave 4 AI-biologics bioproduction demand; a return to high-single/double-digit organic growth with margin recovery justifies a re-rate back toward historical premium multiples, driving a multi-year compounding leg from a trough entry.
strategic.falsifier: Simultaneous China capex collapse and material NIH budget cuts, or DHR/Sartorius/Repligen collectively guiding bioprocessing lower into 2026, would break the cycle-bottoming thesis and flip the strategic sign to trim.

tactical.bear_case: New-build capacity deliveries (record orderbook ~ mid-20s% of fleet) are hitting the water just as Red Sea reopening risk looms; any Suez normalization collapses the ton-mile premium and spot rates gap down, dragging AMKBY below the 200-day toward the low $11s.
tactical.bull_case: GSCPI at 1.25 plus continued Red Sea diversions keep effective capacity tight; a fresh disruption or a restocking bump ahead of tariff deadlines spikes spot rates, and AMKBY reclaims the 50-day ($12.20) quickly on a squeeze.
tactical.falsifier: A decisive Drewry WCI break above its recent range (rates trending up two-plus consecutive weeks) with price reclaiming and holding above the $12.20 50-day would flip this to a clearer buy; conversely a confirmed Suez reopening cratering rates flips it to sell.

positional.bear_case: The positional GSCPI tailwind reverses fast if Suez reopens or demand stalls into a tariff-induced trade slowdown; AMKBY has been range-bound and HOLD for 46 straight sessions, suggesting the market sees no positional edge and the stock drifts with a softening freight tape.

strategic.bull_case: Below-book asset value plus a persistently disrupted trade map (Red Sea, Panama, tariff rerouting) structurally lengthens ton-miles, keeping the cycle elevated longer than bears expect; management's disciplined capital return (buybacks + 3% yield) shrinks the share count into a re-rating.

tactical.bear_case: The name is up ~200% off its lows and near all-time highs; a -2% day could mark the start of a momentum unwind if semi capex headlines turn, and stretched RSI history means mean-reversion pullbacks are common.
tactical.bull_case: RSI at 48 means the recent run has already consolidated — plenty of room to grind higher into earnings; buyers keep stepping in near highs, a bullish tell.
tactical.falsifier: A single-day drop of >5% on non-fundamental news (e.g., China export-control headline) would flip me to a tactical buy; a blow-off spike RSI>75 into a weak book-to-bill print would flip me to trim.

positional.bear_case: At-highs positioning leaves no margin of safety; any WFE cycle disappointment or 2026 guidance trim would hit a fully-priced position hard, and the stock has run far ahead of its 200-day ($1340).
positional.bull_case: Multi-year backlog visibility plus High-NA EUV ramp gives structural support; pullbacks in this name have been shallow and bought, so the up-and-to-the-right trend is the base case.
positional.falsifier: A close below the 50-day ($1690) that breaks the trend, or a >15% drawdown from highs, would flip positional to a buy; a decisive breakdown through the 200-day would flip to trim.

tactical.bull_case: If the rally is driven by a genuine re-escalation of Red Sea/Suez disruption or a surprise spot-rate spike, momentum can extend — Maersk beta to freight headlines is high and RSI at 53 leaves ample room to run before overbought, so chasing the trim here would be premature.
tactical.falsifier: SCFI/WCI spot rates print a sharp week-over-week increase (>10%) or a fresh Red Sea escalation headline, confirming the rally is fundamentally driven — that would flip me to a buy-side tactical score.

positional.bear_case: The golden cross is late-stage — AMKBY has already run from $11.60 to $12.64 and a +5.7% day into it risks a blow-off; if GSCPI is elevated due to transient Red Sea rerouting rather than structural demand, the tailwind reverses as capacity normalizes and the trend rolls over back toward the 200-day.
positional.bull_case: Sustained GSCPI above 1.0 with price holding above a rising 200-day is exactly the regime where shipping equities re-rate; if trade volumes stay firm and rate discipline holds among alliances, the uptrend continues and the golden cross proves durable rather than exhausted.
positional.falsifier: A close back below the 50-day ($12.23) accompanied by GSCPI falling below 0.5 would break the constructive positional setup and flip me to trim-side.

strategic.bear_case: The 2025-26 orderbook is enormous — the global container fleet is growing double-digit percent while volume growth is low single-digit, structurally pressuring rates for years; below-book P/B may be justified if normalized earnings collapse toward the trough, and Maersk's costly logistics/integrator strategy has destroyed capital versus pure-play DSV, so the SOTP 'discount' may be a permanent conglomerate penalty rather than a coiled spring.

positional.bull_case: PBR.A trading near its 200-day with a 9.4% yield and WTI well above breakeven means limited downside and strong carry; a stable dividend declaration plus a firm oil tape could reprice the name toward the $18 SMA50, a ~12% move.
positional.falsifier: A government announcement redirecting cash flow away from dividends toward mandated investment, or BRL breaking above 5.30 on fiscal deterioration (flips to +); conversely a confirmed dividend hike with oil firming would push further negative.

strategic.bear_case: Low trailing P/E confirms peak-cycle earnings; if oil rolls over toward the low $60s while Lula pressures Petrobras to over-invest and cut payouts, both earnings and the dividend re-rate down — the classic value trap for a state oil major.
strategic.bull_case: Pre-salt is among the lowest-cost oil on earth; Petrobras can sustain a double-digit yield through the cycle, and a resolution of political risk (or a market-friendly 2026 election narrative) could compress the persistent EM/state-control discount, re-rating the whole equity.
strategic.falsifier: A structural policy shift — either a durable dividend-policy guarantee / privatization signal (flips negative) or a formal move to mandate lower fuel prices and higher capex at the expense of shareholders (pushes more positive).

tactical.bear_case: RSI 68 near ceiling after a +8.6% 30d run means the easy tactical bounce is spent; a hot CPI or biotech pullback could trigger mean reversion given how far above SMA50 it sits.
tactical.bull_case: Quality bid (QUAL +2.3pp vs SPY) plus biotech thaw momentum could carry TMO through the RSI ceiling as a defensive quality name; low VIX (16.5) supports continued grind-up.
tactical.falsifier: RSI pushing above 72 with price extending >15% above SMA50 on declining volume would flip this to a clear trim.

positional.bear_case: TMO already ran +8.6% in 30d; the XBI thaw is now visible to everyone and partly reflected — booking re-acceleration to 5%+ hasn't yet printed, so the next earnings could disappoint a market that already bid the recovery.
positional.bull_case: XBI 90d +24% is a leading indicator that hasn't yet hit TMO's reported bookings; if Q1 2026 confirms re-acceleration off the ~1% organic trough, TMO catches up to DHR and re-rates over the next 20 days.
positional.falsifier: XBI 90d rolling negative or a biotech funding freeze (IPO/M&A drought) would remove the bookings tailwind and flip positional to neutral/trim.

strategic.bear_case: NIH funding cuts and China life-sciences capex weakness are structural overhangs that could cap the recovery; if organic growth stalls at 2-3% rather than re-accelerating to 5%+, the forward multiple looks full and there is no re-rate.
strategic.bull_case: TMO is a wide-moat compounder buying at trough earnings and compressed margins; as bioprocessing destocking fully clears and Wave-4 AI-biologics bioproduction demand builds, both organic growth and margins inflect, driving EPS and multiple expansion off a low base.
strategic.falsifier: A confirmed simultaneous China capex collapse plus material NIH budget cuts, or bioprocessing commentary from DHR/Sartorius/Repligen turning back toward destocking, would flip strategic to neutral/trim.
